Product: Prepaid card
Issue: Problem getting a card or closing an account
Consumer complaint narrative: On XXXX I received a prepaid debit card from Netspend. I was confused about why, as I had never set up an account with them. I had completed a rebate for a recent purchase at XXXX and they send a prepaid card for their rebates, so I thought maybe that is what it was for. So I called the number to activate the card. When it asked for my social security number I became concerned. Then I did some research and discovered this is a marketing gimmick this company does in order to get people to put money on the debit cards. I called Netspend to confirm that I did NOT have an account and had NOT activated the card. I was put through many cycles of automated menus before I was finally able to speak to someone. When they tried to verify my identity, I discovered that there was an account created under my name and social security number but there was a different email, mailing address, and phone number associated with this account. I deduced that someone had stolen my identity to set up an account with Netspend. I asked them to close my account but they kept saying they could not get into my account because I could not verify the email, phone, or mailing address on the account ( because they belong to whoever stole my identity ). First of all it is not above board to send people debit cards who have never set up accounts, and second, it is not acceptable that I am not able to close a fraudulent account, and whoever stole my identity gets to keep using this account under my name and my social security number.

Consumer complaint narrative:
This is a follow-up to my original complaint filed on XX/XX/XXXX ( Complaint ID : XXXX ) regarding the inaccurate reporting of a XXXX  XXXX XXXX account XXXX XXXX and Equifax. Despite my dispute and the ongoing investigation, significant discrepancies remain in how this account is reported across both CRAs. These inconsistencies, combined with XXXX 's misleading statement that the dispute is " completed, '' violate the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) and cause harm to my creditworthiness. 

Key Reporting Issues 1. Misrepresentation of Dispute Status XXXX : The account is marked as " Completed investigation of FCRA disputeconsumer disagrees, '' yet my CFPB complaint remains active and unresolved. 
Equifax : While the dispute is marked as " in progress, '' the payment history does not reflect the dispute status. 
FCRA Violation : Section 611 ( c ) requires CRAs to mark accounts as " disputed '' until the investigation is fully resolved. Failure to do so misleads creditors. 
Relevant Case Law : XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX ( XXXX XXXX. XXXX ) : CRAs must include a dispute notation on a credit report during an active dispute. 
XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX ( XXXX XXXX. XXXX ) : CRAs are liable for misleading creditors by failing to reflect the dispute properly. 
2. Inconsistent and Incomplete Payment History The payment histories reported by XXXX and Equifax are conflicting and incomplete : Equifax Reporting : XXXX : No data reported XXXX, " 60 days late '' in XXXX. 
XXXX : " 90 days late '' in XXXX, " CO '' ( charge-off ) for XXXX and XXXX, with " No Data '' for other months. 
XXXX : No data for most months. 
XXXX XXXX : XXXX : " On time '' payments XXXX, " XX/XX/XXXX days late '' for XXXX, " CO '' ( charge-off ) in XXXX. 
XXXX : " CO '' ( charge-off ) for XXXX and XXXX, " No Data '' for other months. 
XXXX : " CO '' ( charge-off ) for XXXX, " No Data '' for most months. 
FCRA Violation : Section 605 ( a ) mandates that all reported information must be accurate and non-misleading. The discrepancies between XXXX and Equifax fail this standard. 
Relevant Case Law : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX XXXX. XXXX ) : CRAs must conduct independent investigations to ensure data accuracy and can not rely solely on the furnisher. 
XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX ( XXXX XXXX. XXXX ) : CRAs are liable for failing to use reasonable procedures to ensure reporting accuracy. 
3. Failure to Verify Reporting Accuracy XXXX XXXX XXXX has not provided documentation to substantiate the reported late payments, charge-offs, and delinquencies.

The Fair Credit Reporting Act ( 15 U.S. Code 1681 ) says ( 1 ) The banking system is dependent upon fair and accurate credit reporting. Inaccurate credit reports directly impair the efficiency of the banking system, and unfair credit reporting methods undermine the public confidence which is essential to the continued functioning of the banking system.

This is a violation of my privacy with my consumer report, since I control what is listed and what is not. Consumer complaint narrative:
To Whom It May Concern, I am a co-signer on my son, XXXX XXXX XXXX loans. I became XXXX XX/XX/2010. I spoke with them about taking me off the loan because I had no ability to pay this loan. This began an avalanche of threatening phone calls. I ca n't help my XXXX or my lack of income. You can not work with these people. My son and I spoke with a gentleman at Navient/XXXX about making 12 consecutive payments to have me removed as co-signer. My son set up pre-arranged schedule autodraft payments to satisfy the requirement for taking me off as a cosigner. My son made each and every payment as scheduled by Navient/XXXX. When the 12th payment was complete, we called to have me removed. Each person that we spoke with did not have any record of the arrangement and stated one of the payments was late even though it was scheduled by them to be autodrafted out of my son 's account. 

In addition, I asked for a copy of the individual loans complete with signatures and got some documents but none were signed. I again requested the documents and was told they were not available and even if they were available, that I could not have access to them. 

Some time later XXXX had an agreement with them to make payments at a specific interest rate ( 3 % ). After paying as agreed for a year I received a phone call threatening default. I asked if XXXX had made payments as agreed and why he was behind when they had processed the payments per the agreement. They had no response but that he was behind and if additional payment was not made it would begin legal proceedings. 

The most frustrating part of it all was the lack of communication and the lack of records kept from previous interactions with XXXX/Navient. Each person we would speak to would have record of any of the previous calls/interactions with their company and we would get transferred to multiple departments. The whole time I felt like I was receiving the run around. My son and I tried to continually make arrangements with the company but they had no records of previous communication. We asked for records but they refused to give us anything. We finally gave up and hired a lawyer. Awaiting default/collection notice as the loans defaulted XX/XX/XXXX or XX/XX/XXXX and still not communication.

Consumer complaint narrative:
Dear Sir or Madam, 1. CB/XXXX  Account Number:XXXX Please correct this inaccurate information on my credit report. I recently pulled my credit report from your Bureau and my attorneys and I had a chance to go over my credit report and in amazement, that you have decided to report me 30 days late on this account in XX/XX/XXXX. 


I immediately disputed this information with CB/XXXX and the results of the investigation came back " verified ''. Not only was I never late on this account, but according to the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), the information furnisher is required to notify me of the insertion of negative listings. Since I have disputed the lates with the creditor, and you obviously " verified '' them, I am very curious as to what kinds of " records '' you may have for this alleged account. I am very confident that you are in violation of the ( FCRA ) and ( FCBA ) sections ( 1666, 1666a. 1666b., and 1666c. ) as stated below : 1666. Correction of billing errors ( b ) Billing error ( 4 ) The creditor 's failure to reflect properly on a statement, a payment made by the obligor or a credit issued to the obligor. ( 5 ) A computation error or similar error of an accounting nature of the creditor on a statement. ( 6 ) Failure to transmit the statement required under section 1637 ( b ) of this title to the last address of the obligor which has been disclosed to the creditor, unless that address was furnished less than twenty days before the end of the billing cycle for which the statement is required. ( 7 ) Any other error described in regulations of the Bureau. 1666a. Regulation of credit reports ( a ) Reports by creditor on obligor 's failure to pay amount regarded as billing error After receiving a notice from an obligor as provided in section 1666 ( a ) of this title, a creditor or his agent may not directly or indirectly threaten to report to any person adversely on the obligor 's credit rating or credit standing because of the obligor 's failure to pay the amount indicated by the obligor under section 1666 ( a ) ( 2 ) of this title, and such amount may not be reported as delinquent to any third party until the creditor has met the requirements of section 1666 of this title and has allowed the obligor the same number of days ( not less than ten ) thereafter to make payment as is provided under the credit agreement with the obligor for the payment of undisputed amounts. ( b ) Reports by creditor on delinquent amounts in dispute ; notification of obligor of parties notified of delinquency If a creditor receives a further written notice from an obligor that an amount is still in dispute within the time allowed for payment under subsection ( a ) of this section, a creditor may not report to any third party that the amount of the obligor is delinquent because the obligor has failed to pay an amount which he has indicated under section 1666 ( a ) ( 2 ) of this title, unless the creditor also reports that the amount is in dispute and, at the same time, notifies the obligor of the name and address of each party to whom the creditor is reporting information concerning the delinquency. ( c ) Reports by creditor of subsequent resolution of delinquent amounts A creditor shall report any subsequent resolution of any delinquencies reported pursuant to subsection ( b ) to the parties to whom such delinquencies were initially reported. 1666b. Timing of payments ( a ) Time to make payments A creditor may not treat a payment on a credit card account under an open end consumer credit plan as late for any purpose, unless the creditor has adopted reasonable procedures designed to ensure that each periodic statement including the information required by section 1637 ( b ) of this title is mailed or delivered to the consumer not later than 21 days before the payment due date. Because I take my credit very seriously, I contacted every name and account that was listed on the report. I was again very shocked by the multiple names, account numbers and figures. Included with this letter, is every letter written to those who have hampered my ability to obtain credit. I believe that any judge that looks over the report would agree that it is a violation of both the FCRA, CCPA and FACTA. The following accounts were listed on your report and I have demanded from them a full accounting and five years worth of records to see if their accounts were in violation of the FAIR CREDIT BILLING ACT ( FCBA ). We also demanded signed contracts, agreements or any legal paperwork that would tie us to the following accounts. Under the FCRA, you are required to conduct an investigation on this account if I request it. I DEMAND to see all 100 % verifiable proof that I was indeed Verifiably late on these accounts and the creditor fulfilled its obligations under ( FCBA 1666 ( a ) ( 2 ) ), As I was never notified of a billing delinquency on my accounts, which the creditor must do under section ( FCBA1666 ( b ) ( 6 ). I DEMAND to see proof that I was mailed my statement to the correct address notifying me of both payment due date and delinquency of payment on the account. If you can not provide 100 % Verifiable proof and proper documentation that the CREDITOR fulfilled its obligations under the ( FCBA ) and ( FCRA ), I therefore demand these late payments be removed from my account or I will be forced to be in contact with the Attorney General of my State and the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau. I therefore am submitting my written request to you to conduct an investigation. Per the FCRA, you have 30 days to conduct this investigation and respond to my request. If you do not respond within this time period, per the FCRA, you must remove this negative information. Thank you for your time, XXXX XXXX SS # : XXXX Date of Birth : XX/XX/XXXX BCC : Consumer Financial Protection Bureau XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, Iowa XXXX

Consumer complaint narrative:
I was approved for a COVID forbearance program ( XX/XX/XXXX - XX/XX/XXXX ) by my lender, Huntington ( XXXX, Ohio ). Once the forbearance period was complete, they provided an application for payment readjustment. I completed that and returned it in XX/XX/XXXX. I also began making the regular monthly payments on XX/XX/XXXX. They subsequently had asked me for further documentation to accompany my application ( most of which is not even available ), and I provided an explanation via email why that documentation is not available. When I continually received no response to that particular email, I followed up with at least four emails and a couple telephone calls. Apparently, they decided that my application was incomplete and turned my account over to foreclosure without my knowledge. While they stated that a letter was sent, I DID NOT RECEIVE ANY COMMUNICATION from them -- via mail or to any of my emails -- that my application had been rejected. The ONLY information I received ( by mail ) was another blank application to complete, the same application that I had already completed and was confirmed to have been received by Huntington.

Meanwhile, I had been receiving an occasional notice from the foreclosure department all during the forbearance process. I was consistently told that such notices were normal and to ignore them. Also, my last two payments toward my mortgage were rejected. My last verbal communication with Huntington about the forbearance program resulted in being told that there was nothing that could be done by the forbearance folks because foreclosure had the account. I was also told that I needed to complete another application, apparently for loss mitigation. Meanwhile, they want full payment for the forbearance program along with the two recent payments that were rejected. I did receive, by mail, a letter regarding this, but it came from the foreclosure folks, not the forbearance folks. Unfortunately, I did not immediately react because of all the times I was told it was standard during the entire forbearance process. 

I did complete the loss-mitigation application and sent that via email this morning ( XX/XX/XXXX ). Because I'm not certain how the process works, I am in jeopardy of losing my home. I feel that Huntington was negligent in their communications, and what communication they did provide was ambiguous. I also feel that they have taken advantage of the situation. I am XXXX XXXX XXXX and have significant equity in my home with only 11 years left on my mortgage. If foreclosure would occur, it would only benefit them. 

I find it frustrating ( if not fraudulent? ) that I can be approved for the COVID forbearance program because of a hardship, then expected to immediately pay back that amount as if the hardship never existed. In my experience, the COVID forbearance program presented a beneficial situation for the lender and not the borrower. 

I am attaching the emails I sent to the individual who supposedly oversaw my account. I NEVER RECEIVED ANY COMMUNICATION from her. I think that the lack of communication from Huntington is evident by my consistently trying to reach out to her.

I am submitting a formal complaint regarding MOHELAs mismanagement of my student loan account and XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX ) qualifying payment count. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, after receiving a notice via XXXX that none of my payments from XX/XX/XXXX, through XX/XX/XXXX, would count toward XXXX due to my loans being in forbearance, I immediately submitted a written inquiry through MOHELAs secure messaging portal. I never requested forbearance during that time. MOHELA representatives had previously assured me that any administrative forbearance applied would not impact my PSLF progress and would still count toward my qualifying payments. 

Despite being employed full-time by a qualifying public service employer and submitting my XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, I was told this period would not be counted due to forbearance. I provided written documentation and screenshots to MOHELA to support my request to correct this error. 

MOHELA responded by instructing me to resubmit my XXXX form and use the PSLF Help Tool actions I had already taken. Their response failed to address the central issue : the forbearance status that was applied to my account without my request or consent. 

I followed up again on XX/XX/XXXX, reiterating my concerns and asking for a correction. MOHELA replied stating they required more information and requested I call them. I called and spent nearly XXXX hours on the phone on XX/XX/XXXX, where a representative acknowledged the issue, claimed to have resolved it, and promised my PSLF progress would be updated retroactively and moving forward. She also said my balance during the months in question would have remained {$0.00}, so my XXXX payments should have counted. She further claimed she removed the forbearance and notated my account and would send a confirmation letter. I never received this letter. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I submitted another written request because my MOHELA and XXXX accounts still showed the loans in forbearance. I made it clear that this was my fourth attempt to resolve this issue, and that I would be filing a formal complaint if it remained unresolved. 

To date, my XXXX qualifying payment count only shows XXXX of the XXXX payments. It should show at least XXXX, based on my continued full-time employment with a qualifying employer and meeting all other requirements. XXXX additional payments also appear to be incorrectly marked as requiring employment verification, even though my documentation for that period has already been submitted and approved. 

Throughout this process, I have : Submitted multiple formal written requests via MOHELAs portal Spent hours on the phone with representatives Been promised a supervisor would return my call ( which never occurred ) Been misled or given conflicting information Been left without resolution despite clear documentation and compliance on my end This has caused undue stress and delays in my PSLF forgiveness timeline. I am requesting immediate correction of my PSLF qualifying payment count, accurate removal of the forbearance status for the period in question, and a formal written response acknowledging and confirming the resolution.

In XXXX of XXXX  Suntrust bank made on-line system changes the merged their Mortgage accounts into the same on-line portal that is used for checking account. Starting in XX/XX/XXXX when I took out my mortgage with Suntrust I was eligible to have my checking account fee waived so long as I maintained a minmum daily balance of all combined accounts of {$25000.00}. My mortgage account had well more than that averge daily balance. IN XXXX, XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX, after never having been charged a fee I was charged {$25.00} maintance fees for my checking account. ON our about XX/XX/XXXX I spoke with a representative at Suntrust, who said that the fee was charged in error, that because of the system change my account was not properly linked. I was never notified by mail or electronically of this system change or that any action was required to ensure the fee would not be charged. I was refunded the {$25.00} fee and the representative linked my account. 

In XXXX and XXXX I was again charged the {$25.00} maintenance fee for my checking account. On XX/XX/XXXX I again contacted Suntrust, this time the story changed. I was informed by the representative that ones mortgage account no longer could count towards the {$25000.00} minimum balance fee waiver for signature checking accounts. The representative could not identify when this change occurred or explain why if that was the case I had been receiving a fee waiver for four years. I was refunded ( after a little back and forth and being elevated to a supervisor ) the additional fees I was charged. The supervisor also informed me they do annual checks of all accounts to ensure proper fees are being charged and perhaps in XXXX, which would have been an annual anniversary they saw I was not being charged the fee I should have been. To the extent I had been receiving the fee waiver by accident, at the very least Suntrust should have to notify me of that fact and tell me actions I could take to avoid the fee. I have looked back through my correspondence, which I receive all electronically, and I do not have any notification at anytime over the past four years and certainly not in the past four months when these fees were being charged, that my account was no longer eligible for the fee waiver. The supervisor said that there should have bene a note on my monthly statement. There was no note indicating that there has been a change to my fee waiver status. And perhaps even more disturbing when I commented to the agent that even if there was a note this is the type of thing that should not be buried on a statement as I don't always check them since I review everything via the on-line portal. She agreed and admitted she never actually reads her statements.

Consumer complaint narrative:
I am disputing the accuracy and legal compliance of the collection account listed as : Security Credit Services Balance : {$340.00} This account is being reported in violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act, specifically 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( a ) ( 5 ).

This rebuttal responds to XXXX XXXX and Security Credit Services claim that assignment of the account cures the disputed credit reporting inaccuracies.

While the contract may permit assignment of the agreement, contractual assignment does not supersede or negate compliance with the Fair Credit Reporting Act.

The response confirms the following : The account was charged off on XX/XX/year> The account was purchased by Security Credit Services on XX/XX/year> However, neither party has identified or documented the Date of First Delinquency, which is the controlling date under 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( a ) ( 5 ) and 1681c ( c ). Reporting the account as opened in 2025 based on purchase or assignment date constitutes improper re-aging, which is prohibited by the FCRA.

Additionally, citing an assignment clause does not satisfy the furnishers duty under 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( b ) to conduct a reasonable investigation after dispute. Verification requires review and production of actual account-level records, including payment history and DOFD not contractual language. 

To date, neither XXXX Leasing nor Security Credit Services has provided : The original Date of First Delinquency Complete payment history Records establishing how the reported dates were determined Evidence supporting continued reporting Under 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( a ) ( 5 ) ( A ), information that can not be fully verified may not be reported. Continued reporting without the required documentation constitutes ongoing violations of : 1681s-2 ( a ) ( 5 ) 1681s-2 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( A ) 1681s-2 ( b ) 1681i ( a ) 1681c ( c ) 1681g ( a ) ( 1 ) I am requesting that the CFPB require deletion of this collection account from all consumer reporting agencies due to improper re-aging, incomplete verification, and continued reporting of unverifiable information.

Consumer complaint narrative:
XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX TX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX TransUnion LLC XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, PA XXXX Subject : Formal Notice of FCRA and FDCPA Violations and Demand for Immediate Action Dear TransUnion Dispute Department, I am writing to formally notify you of multiple violations of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) and the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ) concerning inaccuracies and discrepancies in my credit report. Despite multiple disputes submitted to your office, you have failed to correct or adequately investigate these inaccuracies, thereby breaching your legal obligations under federal law. 

Violations of the FCRA and FDCPA : FCRA Violation 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( a ) : This section mandates that credit reporting agencies conduct a reasonable reinvestigation of any disputed information. Your failure to diligently investigate and rectify the disputed entries constitutes a direct violation.

FCRA Violation 15 U.S.C. 1681e ( b ) : You are required to maintain maximum possible accuracy in the information you report. The inconsistencies between my TransUnion report and those from XXXX and XXXX indicate negligence in maintaining accurate records. 

FDCPA Violation 15 U.S.C. 1692e ( 8 ) : It is unlawful for a debt collector to communicate false credit information, including failing to communicate that a debt is disputed. Your inaction suggests willful non-compliance with this statute.

Request for Reinvestigation Details : Under 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( a ) ( 6 ) ( B ) ( iii ) of the FCRA, you are required to provide a detailed description of the procedure used during the reinvestigation process upon the consumer 's request. Therefore, I am formally requesting a thorough list of all sources, documents, and methods used in the reinvestigation of the disputes listed on my credit report. Failure to provide this information will be considered an additional violation of federal law.

Harm and Damages Caused : Your negligence has resulted in significant financial servitude, mental distress, and economic damages. The FCRA under 15 U.S.C. 1681n and 1681o provides consumers the right to seek damages from credit reporting agencies that willfully or negligently fail to comply with the Acts requirements. Your persistent refusal to correct inaccuracies has directly contributed to financial strain, including denied credit opportunities and increased interest rates, compounding both my financial and emotional distress. 

Supporting Case Law : XXXX XXXX TransUnion XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX XXXX. XXXX ) : The court held that a credit reporting agency must go beyond the original source of information when conducting reinvestigations, emphasizing the importance of thoroughness and diligence. 

XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX Cir. XXXX ) : This case underscores the liability of credit agencies when failing to properly investigate disputes, resulting in damages awarded to the consumer. 

XXXX v. XXXX XXXX. XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. LEXIS XXXX ( XXXX. XXXX XXXX ) : XXXX was found liable for failing to conduct a reasonable investigation of a consumers dispute, leading to compensation for the plaintiff. 

XXXX v. TransUnion, XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX Cir. XXXX ) : This case involved XXXX failure to properly investigate and correct inaccuracies, resulting in significant damages awarded to the consumer. 

Demand for Immediate Action : Given the multiple attempts I have made to resolve these discrepancies amicably, I am now left with no choice but to prepare for legal action. Should TransUnion fail to correct the inaccuracies and provide written confirmation within 15 days of receipt of this letter, I will proceed to file a lawsuit to seek full compensation for the financial and emotional damages caused by your negligence and non-compliance with federal law. 

This is my final attempt to resolve this matter outside of court. I strongly urge you to take immediate corrective action and uphold your legal responsibilities under the FCRA and FDCPA. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX all rights reserved

I suspect there are serious issues with how my account is being managed by MOHELA, and given the federal investigation, I am requesting that the CFPB investigate this matter thoroughly.

Providing inaccurate or incomplete account information, in violation of their responsibilities under federal servicing contracts.

Under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), specifically Section 611 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( A ), I am asserting my right to dispute the information MOHELA has provided to credit reporting agencies.

In the absence of verifiable and accurate records supporting the validity of this debt, I demand that MOHELA immediately cease collection activity, correct or delete any erroneous entries reported to credit bureaus, and provide documentation to confirm the lawful status and management of these loans.

I am requesting the CFPB to intervene and investigate this matter thoroughly. I also request that the debt be considered disputed and unverified, and as a result, cancelled, and demand that all reporting be suspended or removed from my credit report under FCRA regulations.

I appreciate your time and attention to this matter, and I am available to provide further documentation, account numbers, or correspondence history if needed. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

Consumer complaint narrative:
This is my second formal complaint regarding Equifax 's failure to remove 2 collection accounts : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX that do not belong to me. In my previous complaint, Equifax acknowledged the possibility that I may be a victim of fraudsomething I clearly stated and provided supporting documentation for, including a filed FTC Identity Theft Report and police report. Despite this, Equifax has continued to report the disputed accounts without removing them or providing any documentation validating their legitimacy or confirming I opened them.

Under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), specifically 1681c-2 ( a ), a credit reporting agency is required to block any information resulting from identity theft within four business days after receiving proper proof, which I submitted with my initial dispute. Equifaxs failure to comply is a clear violation of this provision. 

Furthermore, under FCRA 1681i ( a ), Equifax is required to conduct a reasonable reinvestigation of any disputed item. Simply verifying the debt with the furnisher, without evidence such as signed applications or transaction records, does not meet the standard of a reasonable investigationespecially in cases involving identity theft.

I am requesting the CFPBs assistance in compelling Equifax to permanently delete these fraudulent accounts, which are harming my credit profile and causing financial hardship. I have already submitted : A signed FTC Identity Theft Affidavit A police report A copy of my government-issued ID Proof of address A written statement detailing the loss of my personal information and unauthorized use These collection accounts were not opened or authorized by me. I have no knowledge of them, did not receive goods or services from the alleged creditors, and never signed any agreements. My information was compromised when my purse containing sensitive documents was lost at the airport, which I disclosed in my initial report.

The same accounts are reported with different balances, dates of last activity, dates reported, delinquency histories, and even reporting presence depending on the bureau, which makes it impossible for my credit file to reflect a single accurate financial history. For example, XXXX account XXXX shows a balance of {$470.00} with 30-, 60-, and 90-day late payments on both Experian and Equifax, yet the dates of last activity and reporting differ significantly between bureaus. XXXX XXXX account XXXX appears with a balance of {$1800.00} and multiple delinquency levels across all three bureaus, but each bureau reports different activity dates, and older XXXX-balance versions of the same account show late payments on Experian and TransUnion while Equifax lists the account in good standing. Additional accounts such as XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  ) appear on only one or two bureaus while being absent from others, or show conflicting late payment histories and reporting timelines that can not all be accurate at the same time. These discrepancies indicate failures by furnishers and credit reporting agencies to ensure information is complete, accurate, and consistently updated as required under the Fair Credit Reporting Act, including FCRA 611, 623 ( a ) ( 2 ), 623 ( b ), and 15 U.S.C. 1666 and 1666a. Product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
Issue: Problem with a company's investigation into an existing problem
Consumer complaint narrative:
Dear TD Bank, I am writing to formally lodge a complaint regarding a significant issue that has negatively impacted my credit report and caused a severe drop in my credit score. This situation stems from an instance where I was incorrectly reported as being 30 days late on payments for my TD Cash credit card ending in XXXX. 

The core issue is that I was unable to make payments for this account on time due to restricted online access. I was told by three in-person representatives and during 12 separate phone calls to the TD Credit Card Department that my online account access and login capabilities were restricted, preventing me from making payments through the usual channels. The restricted access was in place for an extended period, and, unfortunately, this happened until it was too late for me to take action and avoid a 30-day late payment on my account.

Once I discovered the issue, I immediately took steps to resolve it. After further follow-up, I was finally connected to a credit card manager who, after escalation, was able to reset my online access. However, by that time, the 30-day late payment had already been reported to the credit bureaus, causing a dramatic XXXX decrease in my credit score. 

I want to make it clear that this situation was not caused by negligence or disregard for my payment obligations. I was fully prepared to pay, but the inability to access my account online, through no fault of my own, severely hindered my ability to take the necessary action. I have communicated the issue multiple times, but the response from TD Bank representatives was insufficient until it was too late to avoid the reporting. 

This situation, as described, is not a case of me failing to make a payment ; rather, it is an issue that was entirely out of my control due to TD Banks systems restricting my ability to pay. As a result, I request the following : Immediate investigation into the incorrect reporting of the late payment to the credit bureaus.

Correction of the late payment on my credit report, as I was not at fault for the delay.

Assistance in mitigating the damage caused to my credit score due to the delayed resolution of this matter. 
I believe this situation was the result of an internal error on TD Bank 's part, and I am seeking a fair resolution. I trust that you will take swift action to correct this issue and assist in restoring my credit standing. 

Please confirm receipt of this complaint and inform me of the steps you are taking to resolve this matter as soon as possible. 

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Details of the Account : Creditor Name : XXXXXXXX XXXX Account Number : XXXX Date of Deletion : XX/XX/XXXX Timeline of Events : XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX, this account was successfully removed from my credit report after a dispute. 
XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX ], I noticed that the account had been reinserted without prior notification or proper documentation as required under Section 611 ( d ) of the FCRA. 
XXXX. I contacted TransUnion to dispute the reappearance, but the issue remains unresolved. 
Violations of the FCRA : XXXX actions appear to violate the following provisions of the FCRA : XXXX. Section 611 ( d ) : Failure to notify me within five business days of re-reporting the disputed account. 
XXXX. Section 611 ( a ) : Failure to conduct a proper reinvestigation of the account before re-reporting it. 
XXXX. Section 623 : Failure of the data furnisher to verify the accuracy of the information before reinsertion. 
Supporting Evidence : To support my complaint, I have included the following documents : A copy of my most recent credit report highlighting the disputed account. 
Documentation showing the accounts prior deletion. 
A copy of my correspondence with TransUnion regarding this issue. 
My identification ( drivers license ) and proof of address ( Credit Card Statement ). 
Requested Resolution : I am requesting the CFPB to investigate this matter and take the following actions : XXXX. Require TransUnion to remove the account permanently from my credit report. 
XXXX. Ensure compliance with FCRA requirements regarding proper notification and validation of reinsertion. 
XXXX. Hold TransUnion accountable for any negligence or non-compliance with federal laws. 
This situation has caused significant harm, including a drop in my credit score and XXXX related to correcting this issue. I kindly ask for your assistance in resolving this matter promptly. 
Thank you for your time and attention. Please feel free to contact me at XXXX or XXXX if further information or clarification is needed. 
Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

Consumer complaint narrative:
Re : Dispute of XXXX XXXX XXXX Account XXXX. XXXX Dear [ Credit Bureau ], I am writing to formally dispute the inaccurate reporting of the XXXX XXXX XXXX account listed on my credit report. Despite my previous attempts to address this matter, the information continues to be reported inaccurately and in violation of my rights as a consumer under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), specifically Section 1681. 

The following issues must be resolved immediately : XXXX. Incorrect Balance Reporting : The account is listed as a charge-off but is still reflecting a balance. This is inaccurate, as charge-off accounts are required to be written off by the creditor as uncollectible. Additionally, charge-offs should be accompanied by the issuance of a 1099-C form if applicable, indicating debt cancellation. Reporting both a balance and a charge-off is misleading and a direct violation of the FCRA. 
XXXX. Verification Process Not Followed : In my previous dispute, I requested a thorough investigation into the accuracy of this account. However, the response I received stated that the information was verified. This response fails to address my specific concerns or provide the evidence necessary to substantiate the validity of the reporting. This does not meet the requirements for proper dispute resolution under the FCRA. 

Given the continued inaccuracies, I am demanding the immediate removal of this account from my credit report. Under FCRA Section 1681i ( a ), I am entitled to a prompt and accurate reinvestigation of disputed information. Failure to comply with these requirements constitutes a violation of federal law. 

As a consumer, I have the right to an accurate credit report. The presence of inaccurate information on my credit report is harmful and may be actionable. I trust that you will take this matter seriously and address it promptly. 

Please provide written confirmation of your investigation and the steps taken to resolve this dispute within XXXX  days of receipt of this letter. If you fail to remove the inaccurate information, I am prepared to escalate this matter to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) and other relevant authorities. 

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. Please contact me at [ your phone number ] or [ your email address ] if further information is needed. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX XXXX Let me know if youd like to make any adjustments or add additional details.

Product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
Consumer complaint narrative:
I am filing this complaint to address several inaccuracies and violations of my rights under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ), and other relevant laws related to my credit report. These inaccuracies have caused me significant harm and frustration, and I demand immediate resolution. 

FCRA Violations ( 15 U.S. Code 1681 et seq. ) : Inaccurate Information Reporting : My credit report contains numerous items, including bankruptcy, evictions, credit cards, and loans, that were decisions made by my former husband during our marriage, where I had no income or involvement in obtaining these accounts. Per 15 U.S. Code 1681e ( b ), credit reporting agencies ( CRAs ) are required to maintain maximum accuracy in their reporting, which has not been upheld.

Dispute Resolution Failures : Under 15 U.S. Code 1681i ( a ) ( 1 ) ( A ), CRAs are required to investigate disputes promptly and verify or remove inaccurate items. I have attempted to dispute these items, but they remain on my credit report without proper investigation or resolution. Furthermore, 15 U.S. Code 1681i ( a ) ( 5 ) requires the deletion of unverifiable information, which has not been adhered to in my case.

Unauthorized Use of Credit Information ( 15 U.S. Code 1681b ( a ) ( 2 ) ) : My former husband 's actions during our marriage, including opening credit accounts and filing for bankruptcy, were done without my explicit consent. Under 15 U.S. Code 1681b ( a ) ( 2 ), CRAs are only permitted to furnish consumer reports for permissible purposes and with proper authorization, which was not obtained in these instances.

FDCPA Violations ( 15 U.S. Code 1692 et seq. ) : Communication Violations : Debt collectors have contacted me regarding debts that were not authorized or created by me, violating 15 U.S. Code 1692c, which regulates communication with consumers.

Validation of Debt : Under 15 U.S. Code 1692g, debt collectors are required to provide proper validation of debts within five days of initial communication. In several cases, this requirement was not fulfilled.

Billing Error Resolution ( 15 U.S. Code 1666b and 12 CFR 1026.13 ) : Items related to accounts opened by my former husband were inaccurately reported as my responsibility. Per 15 U.S. Code 1666b and 12 CFR 1026.13, creditors and CRAs have a duty to correct billing errors, which has not occurred in my case.

Right to Rescission ( 15 U.S. Code 1635 ) : I was not informed of my right to rescind certain transactions that affected my credit during my marriage. This omission is a violation of 15 U.S. Code 1635.

Ongoing Litigation and Systemic Issues : Recent lawsuits and settlements against XXXX and XXXX highlight systemic issues in their reporting practices. These include failure to investigate disputes, inadequate safeguards to ensure accurate reporting, and improper handling of consumer information. My experience aligns with these concerns, further demonstrating the need for immediate corrective action.

I called Chime to try and clarify if it was real and found out they don't make calls to customers and that's when I realized I was scammed. I tried to dispute it with screen shot proof of what I had. I have already filed a police report and contacted Chime again about recovering my funds. I'm hoping they can credit me for the lost amount of money.

You can paste this in the Describe what happened box ( no personal info included ) : In XX/XX/year> I went to a dealership to finance a vehicle through Santander. The dealer generated a retail installment contract dated XX/XX/year> even though the loan was not yet finalized or funded and the dealership was still asking me for income documents into XXXX. Santander then treated that contract date as if the loan was already active and started aging delinquency from a first payment due around XX/XX/year>. 

In mid-XXXX  I went through serious illness and housing hardship and was briefly out of the country, but I stayed in communication with Santander and, when my health allowed, I began making payments in the fall ( for example, a payment of about {$450.00} on XX/XX/year> ). I spoke to multiple representatives and supervisors who told me my account was being notated and that, as long as I continued to show good faith and make payments, repossession should not be an issue. I was also told that some of the scary repo notices were auto-generated and that I did not need to panic. 

Despite those assurances, the vehicle was repossessed around XXXX XXXX. Only after the repossession did a higher-level representative tell me that if I had made a payment in the {$450.00} range earlier, it could have shaved off delinquency days and helped avoid repo. That option was never clearly explained to me in advance, even though I was already paying in that range. I immediately paid what was required to recover the vehicle, brought the account current within a few days, and have remained current since then. 

I later sent a detailed written request asking Santander to review the delinquency dating, internal coding, and how the repo was handled in light of my hardship, communication, and payment history. The company responded that its reporting is accurate and refused to make any changes, even though their internal treatment of the account ( including a prior-repo/high-risk flag ) is now blocking me from obtaining needed transportation.

Product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
Vehicle loan or lease
Consumer complaint narrative: I have been a long time customer of Volvo and Volvo Financial Services over the last six ( 6 ) years and three ( 3 ) vehicles. During that time, I have never had a late payment or negative mark of any kind on my credit. However, when upgrading my lease from my second lease to my third lease, Volvo Financial did not update my current Volvo Financial portal account as it had done in the past. This meant that my auto-pay settings from which all of my previous payments were made never rolled into the new lease ( as had been done in the past ) and the automated payments that I had relied upon in the past were not made. As a result, no payments were submitted for two months which lead to a 30-60 day non-payment event on my credit report. In addition, no attempt was made to contact me in order to let me know that the third lease was behind in payment. It was only when I tried logging into my Volvo Financial Portal account did I realize that my original account was deactivated and I would need to register a new account for the new lease ( a step that I did not have to take in my previous two leases ). Once I registered for a new account, only then did I see that my account was two ( 2 ) months past due. Upon realization, I immediately paid the outstanding amount and set up a new auto-pay. This new requirement for creating an entirely new Volvo Financial Portal account for each lease was not made known to me until it was too late. In fact, when I spoke to my Volvo Dealer at the time of the third lease, he told me that I would NOT have to update my Volvo Financial Portal account. I followed up with him after learning this and he and his team were completely unaware of this new requirement. Volvo Financial has been a wonderful leasing company and this situation does not change that opinion. I realize that I represent a bit of an edge case ( three sequential leases ), but still believe that I shouldn't be held responsible for a change in their technology platform impacting an auto-pay function that was set up and working for the first two leases. Hopefully, this story helps anyone working with Volvo or a customer of Volvo Financial avoid this situation.
Company: Volvo Car Financial Services U.S., LLC

Consumer complaint narrative:
I am submitting this 3rd follow-up complaint regarding multiple entries on my credit report that appear to have been accessed or listed without my authorization or any legally permissible purpose. Despite my previous disputes, these accounts remain on my report, continuing to harm my creditworthiness and misrepresent my true financial standing.

This complaint is submitted pursuant to FCRA 604 ( f ) 15 U.S.C. 1681b ( f ), which clearly states that consumer reporting agencies may not furnish consumer reports to any entity that does not have a permissible purpose. Upon review, I have identified the following entries as unauthorized or impermissibly accessed : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  Balance Owed : {$1000.00} XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Balance Owed : {$32000.00} XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( Additional accounts as listed in previous correspondence attached ) FCRA 604 ( f ) ( 15 U.S.C. 1681b ( f ) ) : No consumer report may be furnished without a permissible purpose.

FCRA 607 ( b ) ( 15 U.S.C. 1681e ( b ) ) : Agencies must maintain reasonable procedures to ensure maximum possible accuracy, including verifying the legal basis for access.

FCRA 609 ( a ) ( 15 U.S.C. 1681g ) : I am entitled to disclosure of sources and permissible purpose for each entry.

FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( a ) ) : Any disputed item must be reinvestigated and corrected or removed if unverifiable, within 30 days.

FCRA 616617 ( 15 U.S.C. 1681no ) : Willful or negligent furnishing of information without permissible purpose creates civil liability.

These accounts were accessed or reported without authorization, and no documentation verifying a permissible purpose has been provided. The continued reporting of these items violates my rights under federal law and materially harms my credit profile.

Consumer complaint narrative: I am filing a complaint regarding the unauthorized access and breach of my educational records, financial aid data, and IRS tax information by the U.S. Department of Education ( DOE ) and the Department of Government Efficiency ( DOGE ). This breach constitutes a direct violation of the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act ( FERPA ) and Title IV regulations, as well as an egregious violation of my personal privacy and financial security. I recently discovered that my sensitive and legally protected records were accessed and potentially shared without my consent. This includes but is not limited to : 1. My academic records and personally identifiable information ( PIl ). 2. My Free Application for Federal Student Aid ( FAFSA ) data, including financial aid history and loan information. 3. My IRS tax transcripts, which were obtained and used without proper authorization. These actions violate my rights under FERPA ( 20 U.S.C. 1232g ; 34 CFR Part 99 ), which grants me the right to control access to my educational records, as well as Title IV of the Higher Education Act, which governs the use and protection of federal student aid information. Furthermore, unauthorized access to my IRS tax data raises serious concerns regarding potential identity theft, fraud, and violations of federal data security standards. Given the severity of this breach and the direct impact on my financial security and privacy, I demand the following corrective actions : 1. Immediate discharge and cancellation of all my federal student loans due to the breach of my financial aid and tax records, which invalidates the integrity of my loan agreements. 2. A formal investigation into this data breach, including details on how my records were accessed, who accessed them, and for what purpose. 3. A full report on the extent of my data exposure and the corrective measures being implemented to prevent future violations. 4. Immediate notification to all major credit bureaus ( XXXX, Experian, XXXX, and XXXX XXXX ) to place a fraud alert and investigate any unauthorized activity that may have resulted from this breach. This breach has caused significant distress and concern regarding the security of my personal and financial data. If my demands are not met within 30 days, I will be left with no choice but to escalate this matter through legal channels, including filing complaints with the Federal Trade Commission ( FTC ), the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ), and pursuing legal action against the responsible parties. I request a written response addressing these concerns and outlining the immediate actions being taken to remedy this situation. Please direct all correspondence to the address listed above. Thank you for your prompt attention to this urgent matter. I look forward to your immediate response and deletion of my Student Loans.

Consumer complaint narrative:
In XX/XX/XXXX, I was approved for the lease on a new XX/XX/XXXX XXXX XXXX. I made a special order for the vehicle and waited nearly two months for the vehicle to be delivered. Before delivery, the lease was approved for a second time. I took delivery of the vehicle in the end of XX/XX/XXXX. I have now had the vehicle for several weeks and during this time I have been harassed and threatened by Volvo Financial. I received a phone call from Volvo Financial after having the vehicle for one week, they proceeded to interrogate me about my income, the vehicles I traded in, who this vehicle was for, etc. The questions were strange, as this vehicle is for myself. I have evidence to back up my income and they questioned it as if they do not believe me. I have provided them with bank statements, pay stubs, tax returns, and my contract of employment, yet they refuse to accept any of this, after the fact that the vehicle has been approved and Ive had it for several weeks and put a {$5000.00} deposit down on the vehicle. The dealership I purchased the vehicle from has been advocating on my part with Volvo yet it has not made a difference, Volvo told the people at the dealership to remind me that the vehicle is not mine. I entered into a contract with Volvo and am relying on this vehicle for transportation in order to get to my job. They are threatening my ability to keep my job, which is 60 miles from my house. I make nearly {$250000.00} per year and this vehicle is well within my means. The payment on this vehicle is less than the two vehicles it replaces. I have been unjustly questioned, harassed, and discriminated against. I have been put through a very unusual level of scrutiny. The implication, however tacit, is that I am somehow lying and I have falsified my income and my pay stubs, bank statements, etc. Volvos refusal to believe me can only be attributed to the fact that I am a high earning woman and they do not believe this. I would like to know how many men Volvo has put through a similar level of scrutiny. Their business practices and the way they have treated a paying customer is absolutely appalling. I have never undergone such harassment from any company when buying a product. I have owned XXXX, a XXXX, a XXXX, an XXXX, etc, and I have never been treated in such an unacceptable manner. 
It would seem as though that a woman who makes {$250000.00} per year is not good enough for Volvo. If only I were a lesser earning man, perhaps that would be more believable for Volvo. Volvo should be ashamed and embarrassed of their discriminatory business practices. I have more than enough income to make the monthly lease payment of this vehicle, but it is becoming increasingly clear that it is not a matter of having the money or not, they seem to be singling me out and do not want me to have this vehicle. 
I have had this vehicle for several weeks now and Volvo has not let up with their demands and threats. This has been an emotionally traumatic experience that no person should be subjected to. They are unjustly threatening to take a vehicle away from me that is my only means of transportation, thus putting my {$250000.00} per year job at risk. I will not tolerate this. Volvo approved this lease not once but twice and I will not suffer any consequences due to negative actions against me on the part of Volvo. Volvo has the power to ruin me financially by taking this vehicle away from me. I am trusting Volvo for the duration of this lease to have transportation and they are abusing that trust and putting me in a precarious position. Volvo is not a company that can be trusted. Their irresponsible and discriminatory behavior must be dealt with. This is further putting my safety at risk because I do not even know if I can put snow tires on the vehicle or not, which I ordered, so I am being forced to drive around in unsafe conditions due to the winter weather in XXXX XXXX already.
Company: Volvo Car Financial Services U.S., LLC

Consumer complaint narrative: There are inaccurate charge-offs and late payments reporting on my credit report, which I believe is misleading and misrepresents the nature of the debt in question. I have received a Certificate of Indebtedness from the creditor, confirming that the debt has been officially canceled. As per IRS guidelines, this cancellation of debt is considered taxable income, not an unpaid debt. According to the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), only accurate and complete information should be reported on a consumer credit report. Since this charge-off and late payments represents a cancellation of debt and not an outstanding obligation, it should not be classified as a charge-off. Consumer complaint narrative:
Date : XX/XX/XXXX This is my third attempt to have this issue resolved. 
I am writing to formally dispute the inaccurate and harmful information regarding my account with XXXX that appears on my credit report. After thoroughly reviewing the account details, I believe there are several errors that not only violate my rights under federal law but have also caused significant XXXX  XXXX. I request the immediate removal of this account from my credit report. 

Issues with Account Reporting : Inconsistent and Inaccurate Charge-Off Reporting : The account is reported as charged off with a balance of {$460.00} written off in XX/XX/XXXX. However, the account is continuously marked as charged off through multiple months in 2023 and 2024, which is inconsistent with standard reporting practices. Under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) - 15 U.S.C. 1681i, this inaccurate and inconsistent reporting is a violation of my right to have only accurate and complete information appear on my credit report. 

Failure to Verify Information : The discrepancies in the reported charge-off status and payment history indicate that proper verification may not have been conducted. Under FCRA ( 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( a ) ), credit bureaus are required to verify the accuracy of any disputed information. The extended and repeated charge-off reporting should be investigated, and if the information can not be verified, it must be removed from my report immediately. 

Misrepresentation of Past Due Amounts and Balance : The report lists varying past-due balances and a continuing charge-off status over multiple months. Under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ) - 15 U.S.C. 1692e, it is unlawful for creditors or collectors to misrepresent the nature or status of the debt. The ongoing charge-off reporting may violate this section of the FDCPA by misleadingly suggesting that I am repeatedly defaulting on the debt, which is inaccurate and harmful to my credit standing. 

Failure to Update Account Information : There has been no indication of recent payments or updates to the account balance, further suggesting a violation of FCRA ( 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ), which mandates that creditors report accurate, complete, and up-to-date information. If no recent activity has occurred, the account must reflect this accurately and not list repeated negative actions over the past XXXX years. 

Request for Removal and Relief : Due to these violations of the FCRA and FDCPA, as well as the resulting stress and hardship this situation has caused me, I respectfully request the immediate removal of this account from my credit report. The inaccuracies, inconsistencies, and failure to follow proper reporting standards have not only hurt my credit score but have also caused significant XXXX XXXX.

XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX, this account was charged a service fee of {$25.00}. For the prior 4 years we were never charged a fee. When we called XXXX, they could not explain the fee and reversed it. The banker we spoke with suggested changing to Total Checking Account so we changed it. Still, we were advised all accounts were linked. 
XXXX. On XX/XX/XXXX account XXXX was charged a {$12.00} fee. This account is in my name and my son XXXX XXXX. This fee didn't make sense because all accounts were linked and had enough funds to avoid a fee. When I called Chase, again they could not explain the fee and reversed it. I did question another account ( listed in # XXXX ) because account XXXX had a higher balance but the one below in # XXXX had a lower balance and was not charged. The banker I spoke with wasn't sure why this happened but informed me the account below ( # XXXX ) was all set. 
XXXX. Last week, I noticed a {$15.00} fee on the checking account we had ( which is mentioned above in # XXXX ). It was a Total Checking Account and the fee could be avoided if minimum daily balance of {$1500.00} was maintained. This account is in my name only. With all accounts linked, this should have not been an issue. I was frustrated this was happening and emailed the Chase branch to express my frustration about this happening. I spoke with the XXXX RI XXXX XXXX ( filling in at the XXXX RI XXXX ) and she said that because I mentioned filing a complaint with the regulator, I would need to speak with the Branch escalation department. Lastly, XXXX informed me that the day this was charged the balance was below {$1500.00}. But the day I was charged the balances between all accounts was well above minimum balance.

NOTE XXXX : For XXXX years, we were never charged any fees. Now, suddenly we are being hit with all these miscellaneous fees which the bank can not explain. 
NOTE XXXX : After speaking with XXXX XXXX XXXX, someone from Chase has tried calling twice. But I am only able to call and speak with this XXXX rep. I have left a long message from her but have not heard back. Consumer complaint narrative:
I am reporting a financial scam that involved my Chase account. I have been locked out of my account and am being held responsible for nearly {$20000.00} in fraudulent transactions after being tricked and used as a financial mule by a scammer named XXXX XXXX from XXXX XXXXXXXX, Texas. 

In XX/XX/XXXX, I reconnected with XXXX, whom I had dated in XXXX. He told me he owned a clothing business and had investors who could help me launch my own XXXX XXXX. He claimed his business account wasnt set up yet, and asked to use my account temporarily for investor funds. I gave him my information in person because I trusted him and believed the story. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, a check for {$19000.00} was deposited into my account. I did not deposit this check and had no idea it was fake. XXXX convinced me the money was from an investor and said it had to be withdrawn quickly for supplier payments. He then drove me to several branches and ATMs where I withdrew cash and handed it to him : {$1000.00} ( ATM ) {$3000.00} ( Chase branch ) {$4800.00} ( Chase branch ) {$10000.00} ( I was tricked into signing a withdrawal slip ) He used my phone while I was at his house the night before and deleted all of our messages and chats with others claiming it was to clear space. I now realize this was done to cover his tracks.

I never kept any of the money. XXXX blocked me immediately after the account went negative and disappeared. He used me to commit fraud and I am now being blamed. Chase has refused to reopen the fraud claim, locked me out of my account, and told me nothing could be done. Ive asked to view security footage from the branches where XXXX accompanied me no investigation happened. I reported it to police, but they said they needed Chase to pursue it first. 

I have lost {$2600.00} of my own funds. I am terrified and feel completely unsupported.

Chase has violated its obligation under Regulation E by failing to properly investigate this fraud. I am requesting the CFPBs help to : Reopen the case Unlock my account Investigate XXXX XXXX scam Retrieve security footage of him at the branches on XX/XX/XXXX Return the stolen funds

Consumer complaint narrative:
On XX/XX/2019, I was emailed a letter of suspension for a mortgage I had applied for with DHI mortgage.The suspension letter contained a listed of documents I would need to provide in order for a credit decision to be made on my file. Some of the documents had previously been provided but one in particular required I visit the county clerks office in a different area of the state. 

My suspension letter includes a required timeframe to produce the documents of 5 days. It was also suggested to me verbally to shop some other lenders who may have different approval requirements. I then arranged to take a vacation day from work to drive to the clerks office but also began to shop other lenders.

Product: Mortgage
Consumer complaint narrative:
This letter is a request to have the derogatory credit reporting related to this account removed. I have gone through numerous layers of bureaucracy and in-house complaint procedures to get this issue addressed without making any progress. 

This loan was originally XXXX of those infamous neg-am loans that reset in the middle of the big credit crisis back in late XXXX early XXXX. We had taken the neg-am option planning to use the time period of the introductory rate to remodel the house. The plan was to refinance into long term financing once the remodel was complete. However, by the time we were ready to refinance it was impossible to get any credit at all. 

We attempted to get a loan modification from Chase but, unbeknownst to us, were put into a situation which later came to be known as " dual tracking. '' The state of California passed a law banning this in, as I recall, early 2010. Chase and several of the other big XXXX banks paid huge fines. Unfortunately, none of the benefits of the new legislation nor any of the money from the big fines paid did anything to help my situation. 

The " dual tracking '' worked like this : On the side visible to us, we had a " single point of contact '' who took our loan mod application, discussed with us our options and told us what we had to do to get the loan modification. Basically, this person crunched the numbers with us, told us we were approved and that we needed to wait until the underwriters were finished with drafting the new agreement before making any payments. We were told that we could not make any payments during that interim or it would cause the loan mod to re-set and that the underwriters would have to start all over again. 

Despite numerous calls to my " single point of contact '' this story did not change. In fact, the longer it went on, the more that person stressed that making a payment would derail the process. 

Then, we were contact by the foreclosure department and told that we had 60 days to pay all of the outstanding payments plus an ungodly amount of late fees and penalties, or they would foreclose on the house. 

Between the " single point of contact '' in the loan mod department telling us that we would be getting our new paperwork " any day now '' and the foreclosure department giving us a hard deadline leading up to foreclosure, we decided to sell the house. Although we sold for much less than the property had appraised for, we did not have to ask for a short sale. 

Now, six years later, we are looking to refinance some loans which originated prior to the financial crisis. Being able to refinance these loans would result in savings of thousands of dollars a month. The negative reporting on the Chase mortgage loan is XXXX of the major obstacles preventing me from completing the proposed refinances. Plus, this reporting is updated every time I request an investigation from the credit bureaus causing this account to appear as if it were new. I 'm told that this single derogatory account is lowering my score by @ XXXX points alone. 

What I am asking Chase to do is report on the mortgage history as if reflecting the loan modification side and not the foreclosure side. You see, I was following the directions given to me by that " single point of contact '' which were that I must not make a payment. I agreed with those instructions and followed those instructions. 

My non-payment during that underwriting period not only reflected the agreement between myself and the loan modification department, I was told that there would be a penalty for making a payment, i.e. total reset of the agreed upon loan modification. 

Please update the credit reporting to show that the account was " paid as agreed. ''

This leads way to civil liability against the credit reporting agency for negligent and willful noncompliance under 15 U.S. Code 1681n, which allows for {$1000.00} per violation, for account listed without my expressed written consent.

I was told that Wells Fargo had paid my homeowners policy in full ( amount of {$1800.00} ) on XX/XX/XXXX ( see attached receipt from XXXX XXXX ). XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XX/XX/year> XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX Equifax Information Services LLC XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX Subject : Request for Immediate Removal of Outdated Information from Credit Report and Compensation for Violations Dear XXXX XXXX XXXX and Equifax Information Services LLC , I am writing to request the immediate removal of outdated information regarding my accounts from my Equifax credit report dated XX/XX/year>. According to the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), negative information such as late payments or collections should not be reported for more than 7 years from the date of first delinquency. 

The accounts in question are : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Since the last activity on these accounts exceeds the 7-year reporting limit, they should no longer appear on my credit report. I kindly request that both XXXX XXXX XXXX and Equifax investigate this matter and take the necessary steps to ensure this outdated information is removed from my credit report promptly. 

Federal and State Law Violations : Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) : Under 15 U.S. Code 1681c ( a ) ( 4 ), the FCRA mandates that negative information such as late payments should not be reported for more than 7 years from the date of first delinquency. The continued reporting of these accounts violates this federal law. 
New York State General Business Law ( GBL ) 380-j : This state law similarly restricts the reporting of adverse information to 7 years. The ongoing presence of these outdated accounts on my credit report is in direct violation of this statute. 

In addition to the removal of this outdated information, I am seeking compensation for each violation of my rights under both state and federal laws. The continuous reporting of this outdated information has caused significant harm to my creditworthiness and financial well-being over an extended period. 

Based on these violations and the harm caused, I am demanding compensation in the amount of {$10000.00} per violation. Given there are four violations, the total compensation requested is {$40000.00}. This amount is intended to cover damages related to the negative impact on my credit report, financial distress, and any other related inconveniences. 

Please confirm in writing that this correction has been made and provide details on the compensation for the violations incurred. If this matter is not resolved to my satisfaction, I will consider pursuing further legal action. 

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. I look forward to your swift response. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX XXXX

I was also informed that Wells Fargo was not obligated to negotiate any items under a deceased party 's name absent the " certificate '', would not accept a certified copy of the probated will or any other documentation apart from the " certificate '', and would not accept the deposit. I request CFPB scrutiny under TILA, state fraud statutes, and unjust enrichment doctrine. 5. Failure to acknowledge sub
mitted Allonge modifying instrument : I lawfully submitted an Allonge to the original contract pursuant to UCC 3-204 and 3-305, amending the instrument to reflect updated terms and beneficial interest.
