Product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
Consumer complaint narrative:
In reviewing my recent credit reports from the national consumer reporting agencies, I discovered multiple accounts reported inaccurately, inconsistently, and without proper validation or substantiation. I am filing this complaint because the credit bureaus and furnishers involved appear to have failed to comply with the accuracy, reinvestigation, and documentation requirements set forth under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) and the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ). Despite attempts to request validation and clarification, I have not received original documentation proving the accuracy or legitimacy of the disputed accounts. This situation continues to cause significant harm to my creditworthiness, access to financial services, and overall consumer reputation. 

Background Summary The disputed accounts include a combination of revolving credit, installment loans, and collection accounts that appear across one or more of the major bureaus. The items in question contain errors regarding reporting status, delinquency history, balance, and ownership, and in some cases, they appear only on one bureauraising concerns about inconsistent data transmission or unverified furnishing practices. Additionally, several accounts show contradictory statuses such as paid yet also marked as derogatory or collection/charge-off, which is contradictory under FCRA 602As requirement of maximum possible accuracy.

The following accounts are specifically in dispute due to questionable validation or inconsistent reporting : A collection/charge-off entry reported under the furnisher name XXXX, dated XX/XX/XXXX, with a past-due balance of {$360.00}. 

A derogatory tradeline from XXXX XXXX XXXX, closed XX/XX/XXXX, showing a balance of {$8300.00} on TransUnion only. 

A collection item reported by XXXX XXXX, with a past-due balance of {$2200.00}, appearing only on TransUnion. 

A closed XXXX XXXX revolving account that was fully paid yet continues to show 30- and 60-day late payments despite evidence of resolution. 

A closed XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX account, reported as Paid but with prior delinquencies inconsistent with payment history. 

A XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX account closed in XXXX that continues to show a 30-day late marker well beyond the seven-year reporting period allowed by law. 

Chronological Summary of What Happened Over the past several months, I noticed derogatory reporting patterns affecting my credit file. I began by pulling updated credit reports from Experian, TransUnion, and Equifax to compare entries. Upon review, I found multiple inconsistencies between the three bureaus regarding the same accountsdifferences in status, balance, and date of last activity. Some accounts appear only on one bureau, suggesting that data was never validated or uniformly transmitted. This disparity demonstrates a breakdown in accuracy procedures required by FCRA 607 ( B ), which obligates consumer reporting agencies to ensure the maximum possible accuracy of data in each file. 

I then filed formal written disputes with the credit bureaus, identifying each questionable account and requesting documentation to validate the information. Specifically, I asked for : The method of verification used to confirm accuracy. 

The original contracts or legal instruments authorizing the reporting. 

The data sources, system timestamps, and investigation notes supporting verification. 

Confirmation of whether each verification was conducted through e-Oscar, manual review, or third-party submission. 

Copies of communications between the bureau and the furnisher regarding my disputes. 

Despite those specific requests, I received only generic confirmation letters stating that the items were verified or remain. No detailed evidence, signed agreements, or verifiable audit trails were provided. This violates the intent of FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 6 ) ( B ) ( iii ), which requires the bureau to disclose the method of verification when requested by a consumer after a reinvestigation. Without such transparency, there is no assurance that any true investigation occurred.

Impact of the Failures to Investigate and Verify The continuing publication of unverified or inconsistent data has negatively impacted my credit profile and creditworthiness. These inaccuracies affect my ability to qualify for favorable loan terms, access housing or auto financing, and maintain fair insurance rates. The damage is both reputational and financial. Moreover, by failing to provide documentation showing chain of custody, assignment, and ownership for debt-purchased accounts, the furnishers appear to be reporting without legal authority or validation, violating FDCPA 809 ( b ) and 807 ( 8 ), which prohibit communication of unverified or false credit information.

Detailed Account Analysis 1. XXXX ( Collection/Charge-off, Closed XX/XX/XXXX ) This item appears only on TransUnion and not on Experian or Equifax. The account is listed as a collection with a past-due balance of {$360.00} and a status of derogatory. There is no clear indication of the original creditor, and the absence of parallel reporting across bureaus indicates lack of validation. This may constitute reporting without verified documentation, violating FCRA 607 ( B ). Furthermore, the failure to provide evidence of a consumer contract or proof of assignment suggests possible data furnishing without permissible purpose. 

2. XXXX XXXX XXXX ( Closed XX/XX/XXXX ) This item is labeled Derogatory with a balance of {$8300.00}. It appears only on TransUnion, not on Experian or Equifax. The reporting of an account on a single bureau more than six years after closure raises serious compliance concerns. The Fair Credit Reporting Act limits the reporting of negative information to seven years from the original delinquency date ( FCRA 605 ( a ) ( 5 ) ). Given the closure date, this entry may soon exceed the legal reporting period. The bureau has not supplied any proof that it verified this account through manual review or obtained any original contract from the creditor. 

3. XXXX XXXX ( Collection/Charge-off, Past Due {$2200.00} ) XXXX XXXX is a well-known debt buyer rather than an original creditor. Debt buyers are required under FDCPA 809 ( b ) to validate ownership and the full chain of assignment before collecting or furnishing data. The bureau has not produced any purchase agreement, assignment documentation, or chain-of-custody evidence showing that XXXX XXXX legally owns or controls the debt. Continued reporting of such unverifiable data constitutes deceptive practice under 807 ( 8 ) ( communicating credit information which is known or should be known to be false ). 

4. XXXX XXXX ( Closed XX/XX/XXXX, Paid, Late History Reported ) This account shows as paid yet reflects multiple late payments ( 30-day 2, 60-day 1 ). Such inconsistencies misrepresent the consumers payment behavior. No documentation proving that these late entries are accurate has been provided. If the bureau relies solely on automated furnisher responses through e-Oscar, that does not qualify as a reasonable reinvestigation under FCRA 611 ( a ). The continued presence of those delinquencies, despite the account being fully satisfied, creates a misleading and punitive record. 

5. XXXX ( Closed XX/XX/XXXX, Paid ) This account shows a XXXX balance but retains delinquency notations inconsistent with final payment records. It should either reflect paid as agreed or be removed altogether if no documentation can substantiate the late payments. This inconsistency may misrepresent repayment behavior and violates the principle of maximum accuracy under 607 ( B ). 

6. XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( Closed XX/XX/XXXX ) The reporting of a XXXX0-day late mark from XXXX appears to exceed the allowable reporting duration if based on a delinquency prior to that date. The bureaus have not produced the Date of First Delinquency ( DOFD ), which is critical for compliance with FCRA 605 ( a ) ( 5 ). If the DOFD precedes XXXX, this account should have already aged off the file. 

Discrepancies Between Bureaus The most alarming issue is the inconsistency of these accounts between bureaus. Some appear only on TransUnion, others on Experian or Equifax. The presence of certain accounts on one report but not another suggests unvalidated data exchange, incomplete communication, or improper furnishing under FCRA 623 ( a ). Bureaus are obligated to ensure that data supplied by furnishers is uniform, accurate, and reflective of verified records. These inconsistencies create a materially misleading representation of my credit history. 

Failure to Provide a Reasonable Reinvestigation The credit bureaus responses to prior disputes consisted of brief statements indicating that the accounts were verified or updated. None included documentation of the verification process, identity of the furnisher representative, or audit logs demonstrating that human review took place. This approach relies heavily on automated e-Oscar codes, which the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau has previously flagged as inadequate for full compliance with FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 1 ). A proper reinvestigation requires actual evidence, not an electronic confirmation code.

Audit Trail and Data Source Transparency I have repeatedly requested an audit trail showing how the disputed data was obtained, transmitted, and verified. This includes user IDs, timestamps, and system logs indicating when furnishers reported updates. None of this information has been supplied. The absence of a documented trail impairs accountability and violates FCRA 609 ( a ) ( 1 ), which grants consumers the right to all information in their files and the sources of that information. 

Pattern of Procedural Negligence The lack of validation, incomplete reinvestigation, and inconsistent data maintenance demonstrate a systemic problem in the bureaus compliance procedures. The bureaus appear to rely on furnishers data without adequate oversight or verification of original documentation. Such practices undermine the statutory purpose of the FCRAto ensure fairness, accuracy, and privacy in consumer reporting. Each of these failures compounds harm to the consumer and perpetuates false narratives about creditworthiness.

Violations and Legal Provisions Implicated FCRA 602 ( A ) Failure to maintain fair and accurate reporting.

FCRA 607 ( B ) Failure to maintain reasonable procedures to ensure maximum possible accuracy.

FCRA 611 ( a ) Failure to conduct a reasonable reinvestigation of disputed information.

FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 6 ) ( B ) ( iii ) Failure to disclose method of verification.

FCRA 623 ( a ) ( 3 ) Failure of furnishers to report disputed information as disputed.

FCRA 605 ( a ) ( 5 ) Reporting negative information beyond the seven-year limit.

FDCPA 807 ( 8 ) Communicating credit information known or should be known to be false.

FDCPA 809 ( b ) Failure to validate debt prior to continued reporting.

Consumer Harm and Emotional Distress Due to these inaccuracies, I have faced credit denials, higher interest rates, and emotional distress caused by repeated rejections and fear of being viewed as a high-risk borrower. The persistence of these derogatory entries undermines the integrity of my financial profile and violates the trust consumers are entitled to under federal consumer protection laws. Repeated attempts to resolve the issue directly with the bureaus have proven futile due to generic automated responses and lack of transparency. 

Requested Resolution I am seeking the following corrective actions : A full, manual reinvestigation of each disputed account, conducted in compliance with FCRA 611 ( a ), including contact with furnishers to obtain original documentation.

Written disclosure of the method of verification used for each account, including data sources, audit logs, and communications with furnishers.

Immediate removal or suppression of any account that can not be validated with original signed contracts or proof of ownership.

Written confirmation that the bureaus have notified all furnishers of the disputes and that unverifiable data will not be reinserted unless certified under 611 ( a ) ( 5 ) ( B ).

Updated copies of my credit reports reflecting all deletions or corrections within five business days of resolution.

Assurance that any account determined unverifiable will be deleted permanently and not re
-reported without certified validation.

Broader Implications and Need for Oversight This case reflects a broader issue within the credit reporting system, where consumers disputes are frequently handled through automated codes without genuine examination of original evidence. Such procedural shortcuts create systemic inaccuracy, leaving consumers with long-lasting harm. The FCRA and FDCPA were enacted precisely to prevent such negligence, yet their enforcement depends on oversight from agencies such as the CFPB.

By submitting this complaint, I am requesting CFPBs intervention to ensure compliance by both the credit bureaus and the data furnishers. I also request that the CFPB require the involved entities to supply proof that their reinvestigation procedures meet the legal standard of reasonableness and that they retain documentation for each verification claim.

Conclusion In summary, the disputed credit entries are unsupported by verified documentation and exhibit multiple compliance deficiencies under the FCRA and FDCPA. The bureaus reliance on automated verification and their failure to disclose evidence of validation constitute a breach of their statutory duties. I have made multiple attempts to resolve this directly with the bureaus through written correspondence and have provided supporting documentation highlighting discrepancies. Nonetheless, these accounts remain on my credit reports without proper validation, causing ongoing financial and reputational harm. 

I respectfully request that the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau review this complaint, compel the credit bureaus and furnishers to produce full validation documentation, and enforce appropriate corrective action to ensure compliance with all applicable sections of the FCRA and FDCPA. My ultimate goal is fair and accurate reporting based on verified facts, not assumptions or automated data transfers. I am seeking accountability, transparency, and full adherence to the laws designed to protect consumers like me.

Inquiry Details : Ask the creditor to provide specific details about the inquiry, such as the date it was made, the purpose of the inquiry and the type of credit product or service associated with the inquiry. 
Application Documentation : Request copies of any credit applications or other documents related to the inquiry. This may include application forms, agreements, or disclosures that you would have completed or received when applying for credit. 
Authorization Documentation : Ask the creditor to provide evidence of your authorization for the inquiry. This could include signed consent forms, agreements, or disclosures that demonstrate you provided permission for the creditor to access your credit report. 
Communication Records : Request copies of any correspondence or communication between you and the creditor regarding the inquiry. This may include emails, letters, or notes documenting your interactions with the creditor.

Internal Records : Ask the creditor to provide internal records or notes related to the inquiry, including any decision-making processes or reasons for initiating the inquiry. 
Identity Verification : If identity theft is a concern, request verification measures taken by the creditor to confirm your identity before initiating the inquiry. This may include verification of personal information or identity documents provided during the application process. 
Compliance Documentation : Inquire about the creditor 's compliance with relevant laws and regulations governing credit inquiries, such as the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) and Equal Credit Opportunity Act ( ECOA ). ''

Product: Mortgage
Issue: Applying for a mortgage or refinancing an existing mortgage
Consumer complaint narrative:
On approximately XX/XX/XXXX I toured a condo in XXXX, Virginia and decided to put in an offer. I was preapproved by XXXX XXXX XXXX and the initial paperwork was drawn up. As i knew that there would be the possibility I might begin an additional job ( on top of the job i have had since XXXX and applied with income from ), I was clear with the lender about documentation, income, debt, and my timeline. All was smooth and accepted by the seller, and my closing date was set for XX/XX/XXXX at XXXX. 
At XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX ( literally the middle of the night, 11 hours before my closing appointment ), my loan originator called me to request updated paystubs, a new homeowner 's insurance policy, and several other pieces of information ( childcare forms ) in order to close. As this was not the first property I have purchased, i was aware that the loan originator is required to be clear to close by the underwriter in order to schedule closing, so I was confused as to what other information they could need. I got out of bed and sent the requested documents immediately so that I could close. I did not hear back from my loan originator with XXXX  until noon the following day, when he told me the documents were under review by the underwriter. 

This is when i started splitting time between sleeping in my car and renting cheap motel rooms/crashing on friend 's couches in order to stay employed. To date, this delay has cost me roughly {$3000.00}. 

As a result I filed an extension for 30 days which i was relieved was accepted by the seller. Every day of that month i called, texted, and emailed my loan officer and received about one or two replies per week, always asking for new information like updated contracts and w2 forms from the new job i had now begun as a result of the delay in closing. He would also send updated contracts or closing documents that varied wildly in cash to close amount, and never made the adjustments for errors that I would catch in reviewing before signing. He also constantly blamed the Underwriters for taking so long or not responding to him. On several occasions he would ask for a document, i would send it within ten minutes, he would confirm receipt, and then ask me for it again 5-7 days later, only to have me walk him through his own email and see the document had been in his possession for a week without going to the underwriter.

On the 30th day of the extension ( XX/XX/XXXX ) I found another lender who got me preapproved and a lower ( and far more accurate ) cash to close amount, and has already conducted several inquiries with my HR department and my unit. As such, I sent a letter to my loan officer giving him and his underwriter a deadline of Monday, XXXX at XXXX EST to either release my appraisal or send me the clear to close. At XXXXXXXX XXXX I sent them a final email stating that since I had not heard anything I would like to submit the formal letter requesting transfer of my appraisal and loan information to my new lender before the end of the day. The old loan officer with XXXX emailed me and said " we just heard back from the underwriters that they have spoken to your contract manager at your job, and they are aware time is critical ''. This confirms to me that he received my request to transfer and blew it off in favor of going back to the underwriters. 

As of today ( XX/XX/XXXX ), I have not heard anything from XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX as to whether or not they have processed the loan transfer, disapproved my loan application, or cleared me to close. 
I feel that the Veteran 's Administration and any buyers or sellers that use the VA should be warned against doing business with XXXX XXXX XXXX, and possibly review the affiliation they have with this team.

Despite submitting multiple disputes to the credit reporting agencies ( Equifax, TransUnion, and Experian ), the fraudulent accounts remain on my report and are being inaccurately verified as belonging to me. This is a violation of my rights under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) and the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ).

Key Issues : Identity Theft/Fraudulent Accounts : The accounts listed do not belong to me. I have never opened them, authorized them, or conducted any business with these creditors. They are a result of fraud or identity theft. 

Failure to Investigate : I filed disputes with each credit bureau, but the responses I received were generic and claimed the accounts were " verified '' without providing any proof, contracts, applications, or original records to support the claim. This violates FCRA 1681i, which requires a thorough and meaningful investigation.

No Proof Provided : The furnishers and credit bureaus have failed to provide documentation that verifies these accounts as mine. Under the FCRA, if a creditor can not verify the account with actual documentation, it must be deleted immediately. 

Emotional and Financial Distress : These fraudulent accounts have damaged my credit scores, denied me financial opportunities, and caused me significant emotional distress, financial strain, and reputational harm.

This debt occurred during the time my now ex-husband and I had just buried a child, he lost his well paying job, and it was a decision to pay either the light bill or my credit card bill from one income. However, in XX/XX/XXXXwhile speaking to an agent, I advised them I wished to pay my XXXX dollar debt. In doing so, I was transferred to a supervisor in the collections department. After speaking to the gentleman, he advised me that I could no longer pay the debt because it was charged off to a 0 balance and I was set to receive a 10-99C form in the mail, in which I did by the month of XX/XX/XXXX. After receiving that information, I made my loan officer aware of the situation. Months went by and my credit began to improve, but through another XXXX XXXX credit check in the month of XX/XX/XXXX, my loan officer noticed where I needed to pay Discover in order to get a credit rescore, to add a possible XXXX points. It was then that I reminded her, that paying discover could not happen due to the first failed attempt, and it having to be claimed as income, due to the 10-99 C form. She then advised me to contact them, let them know what's going on, and to request a letter from them stating the account was closed and charged off, in which Discover agreed to do, but the letter was never received via fax or through mail, as stated and agreed it would be. My loan officer then decided it was a good idea to call Discover, because they are reporting an unpaid balance to the credit bureaus, showing as if I could pay to clear it up. So on the evening of XX/XX/XXXX, my loan officer and I got on a conference call with Discover, attempting to resolve the issue. After being transferred through three different people, the third and final person, tried to explain the account would not show a zero balance as it wasn't paid off, however it was wrote off. He also began to state, that I could send a check voluntarily to show the zero balance, and to still file the 10-99C form because it wouldn't be retracted upon payment. My concern is simple, when attempting to pay, understanding it wouldn't be totally removed from my credit history, just the zero balance, I was advised of this write off and 10-99C form. Consumer complaint narrative:
My mortgage was sold to this company in mid XX/XX/XXXX. I received both an email and a print letter from my old mortgage company ensuring me that I would not experience any disruptions in my payment plan. I paid my mortgage on the XXXX of the month with my old servicer. I allowed one week to pass and noticed on XX/XX/XXXX that no payment had been applied to my new Mr. Cooper account, which indicated that a payment was due and would be late the following week. On XX/XX/XXXX I made a payment through their website. On Monday XX/XX/XXXX, two of my full mortgage payments plus additional principal totaling {$1400.00} were deducted from my account. 

My husband called Mr. Cooper immediately on XX/XX/XXXX, explained the situation, and asked what we need to do to have one of the charges reversed. They said that they could not to anything until both payments had posted and to call back. On XX/XX/XXXX after the payments had processed he called back. The Mr. Cooper rep told us that this happened because we have two auto pays set up when it is clear on their website that we have XXXX, but that they could refund one of the charges. My husband, the Mr. Cooper customer service rep, and a rep from my bank got on a conference call. Our bank rep confirmed the account numbers and the duplicate charges. The Mr. Cooper rep assured us that our refund was being processed and we would see it in 24-48 hours. 

After 48 hours had passed, we called back to inquire about why we hadn't seen our refund yet. The person explained that we must have been confused and that our refund was processing but it would actually take three to five business days. 

On the fifth business day since our initial request, we still had no refund so we called again. We spoke to a new representative who told us that no refund was processing on our account and that the person we spoke to the first time made a note on the account but " didn't know what she was doing '' so the process had not even been started. At this new person 's request, my husband went to the bank and got a partial bank statement showing the duplicate charges ( which our bank had already verified on XX/XX/XXXX ). He was instructed to send them in to a generic email address and told that whoever saw them would " know what it was for ''. We were told we would see our refund within two business days. It is now the second day and it is clear that nothing has been initiated on their end. They are holding {$1400.00} of our money hostage despite our multiple attempts to resolve this and complete compliance with their repetitive demands. 

On XX/XX/XXXX I sent the company an email explaining that if my situation was not resolved quickly I would be submitting a complaint to the CFPB. They replied quickly to my email stating that they were sorry for the poor customer service and would be calling me to discuss. They never called so I am submitting this complaint two days later as promised.

15 U.S.C. 1681s2 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( A ) Furnishers of information ( like loan servicers or background data providers ) must not report information they know or have reason to believe is inaccurate. 

Judicial Precedents XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX  ) : The Court held that a consumer need not prove that the incorrect information was published to a third party to recover actual damages under 1681i ( a ).

Consumer complaint narrative:
Subject : Formal Complaint for Violation of FCRA and Demand for Immediate Removal of Reinsertion Account Dear CFPB, I am filing a formal complaint against TRANSUNION for violating my rights under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), specifically the reinserted account listed below. This account was previously deleted from my credit report, and I am now demanding its immediate removal due to violations related to the reinsertion of the account without proper verification or notification. 
Account Information : * Account Name : XXXX  XXXX * Account Number : XXXX * Original Report Date of Removal : XX/XX/2025 * Date of Reinsertion : XX/XX/2025 Background : On XX/XX/2025, I disputed an account with TRANSUNION due to inaccurate information. After a thorough investigation, TRANSUNION removed the account from my credit report on XX/XX/2025, confirming its deletion. I have attached proof of this deletion notice. 
However, I discovered on XX/XX/2025, that the same account was reinserted into my credit report. This reinsertion occurred without any proper certification or written notification of the accounts accuracy and completeness, as mandated by the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) under

Section 611 ( a ) ( 5 ) ( B ). FCRA Violations : 1. Violation of Section 611 ( a ) ( 5 ) ( B ) - Reinsertion of Disputed InformationThis section prohibits the reinsertion of deleted items unless the information provider certifies that the information is accurate. TRANSUNION failed to provide such certification, which constitutes a violation of my rights. 
2. Violation of Section 623 ( a ) ( 3 ) - Duty to Correct and Update InformationThe reinsertion of the account without correcting the underlying inaccuracies breaches this section, which requires that information furnishers update and correct inaccurate data. 
3. Violation of Section 611 ( d ) - Notification of ReinsertionTRANSUNION failed to notify me in writing within five business days of the reinsertion of the previously deleted item, violating my right to timely notification under this section of the FCRA. 
4. Violation of Section 607 ( b ) - Maximum Possible AccuracyThe reinsertion of inaccurate information, as well as the lack of procedures to ensure maximum possible accuracy in consumer reports, is a violation of the FCRA. 
Harm Caused : The reinsertion of this inaccurate account has caused significant harm, including : * Damage to my credit score * Increased stress and time spent addressing this issue * Negative impact on my ability to secure a mortgage This issue has severely affected my ability to pursue homeownership for my family, and it remains unresolved due to the incorrect information on my credit report. 
Request for Immediate Action : I request the following actions be taken immediately by TRANSUNION and through your offices assistance : 1. Immediate removal of the reinserted account from my credit report. 
2. Written confirmation that the account has been removed and will not be reinserted unless properly verified.

3. A thorough investigation into TRANSUNION s procedures surrounding the reinsertion of previously deleted accounts.

4. Assurance that my rights under the FCRA will be respected and protected moving forward.

5. Compensation for the damages I have suffered, including adverse effects on my credit score and financial standing. 
Please acknowledge receipt of this complaint and confirm the actions you will take to address this issue. I trust that this matter will be resolved promptly and within the timeframe stipulated by the FCRA.

Consumer complaint narrative:
XXXX XXXX XXXX : XX/XX/XXXX Email : XXXX XXXX ( Last XXXX ) : XXXX Date : XX/XX/XXXX Subject : Final Demand Failure to Verify Within FCRA 30-Day Limit / Request for XXXX. XXXX XXXX Account To : TransUnion / Experian / Equifax To Whom It May Concern, This letter serves as a formal final dispute notice and demand for the Method of Verification ( M.O.V ) regarding the XXXX XXXXXXXX account ( # [ Last XXXX or full if known ] ) being reported on my consumer credit file. It follows my original dispute, submitted to all three credit bureaus and the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) portal, referencing multiple factual inaccuracies and material inconsistencies. 

RECAP OF DISPUTE HISTORY & YOUR FAILURES TO COMPLY TransUnion received my dispute on XX/XX/XXXX, and responded on XX/XX/XXXX 43 days later. 

Equifax received the dispute on XX/XX/XXXX, and responded on XX/XX/XXXX 31 days later. 

Experian received the dispute on XX/XX/XXXX, and responded on XX/XX/XXXX 58 days later. 

Per FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( A ) ( 15 U.S. Code 1681i ), you are required to complete a reinvestigation within 30 days of receiving a consumer dispute. You all exceeded the statutory timeframe, making your so-called " verifications '' legally invalid.

LEGAL REQUIREMENTS YOU VIOLATED Failure to Reinvestigate in 30 Days FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( A ) requires credit bureaus to complete investigations within 30 days of receiving a dispute.

FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 5 ) ( A ) ( i ) : If the disputed item can not be verified, the CRA shall delete the item from the file of the consumer.

CFPB.gov disputes are treated no differently than direct disputes. All disputes submitted via the CFPB portal are still governed by the same legal timeframes and requirements under the FCRA. The 30-day rule applies equally.

Failure to Provide Method of Verification ( M.O.V. ) As required under FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 6 ) ( B ) ( iii ) and FCRA 611 ( a ) ( 7 ), upon completion of reinvestigation, the consumer has a legal right to : a description of the procedure used to determine the accuracy and completeness of the information, including the business name and address of any furnisher of information.

I explicitly requested M.O.V. in my original dispute and have not received this information from any of you.

Furnisher Violations Inaccurate and Incomplete Reporting Under FCRA 623 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( A ), a furnisher may not provide information if it is known to be inaccurate.

The payment history, account status, last activity dates, balance reporting, remarks, and months reviewed fields all conflict across your reports.

These discrepancies are material, misleading to lenders, and damaging to my creditworthiness and Debt-to-Income ( DTI ) analysis. 

APPLICABLE CASE LAW & PRECEDENT XXXX XXXX TransUnion XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX XXXX. XXXX ) CRAs must do more than simply parrot information from furnishers. They are required to perform reasonable investigations. 

XXXX v. Equifax, XXXX. XXXX Establishes that failure to verify disputed data within 30 days under the FCRA necessitates removal. 

XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX Cir. XXXX ) A CRAs failure to delete unverifiable information is a violation of the FCRA and is grounds for civil action. 

FINAL DEMAND REQUIRED ACTION I hereby demand the following : Permanent deletion of the XXXX XXXX account from all three credit bureaus ( TransUnion, Experian, Equifax ) due to : -Untimely reinvestigation -Failure to provide Method of Verification -Ongoing inaccurate and unverifiable reporting -Failure to comply with multiple sections of the FCRA If you refuse to delete, then I demand : Full and complete Method of Verification per FCRA XXXX ( a ) ( XXXX ) : including the name, address, and phone number of the furnisher, and a detailed description of the procedure used to verify this account. 

Cease and desist from any future reporting or updating of this XXXX XXXX account. 
At this point, you are legally prohibited from reporting inaccurate, unverifiable, or untimely verified data. 

You can not correct or update an account that was never validly verified to begin with. Only permanent deletion is allowed. 

NOTICE OF INTENT TO SUE If this account is not permanently deleted within 15 days, I will proceed with formal legal action under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) for the following violations : -Failure to complete reinvestigation within the required timeframe -Failure to provide Method of Verification -Continued reporting of inaccurate and unverifiable information -Willful noncompliance with federal law Damages may include statutory penalties, actual damages, punitive damages, and attorney 's fees as provided under the FCRA. 

Respectfully, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX : XX/XX/XXXX

Consumer complaint narrative:
On XX/XX/2022, we closed on a refinance of our home, taking out additional funds to pay off our creditors and consolidate our bills all to our mortgage. 

We refinanced our existing loan through our current mortgage providers, Freedom Mortgage Corporation. XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  ) was the company who processed most of the mortgage. They were given the cash funds for our creditors to be dispersed into checks. 

They dispersed 8 checks to us, from their XXXX XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX and all 8 checks were delivered to us via XXXX. Unfortunately, addresses with po box , which is every creditor we had, can only be mailed to by USPS. Our checks were stolen from the post office, we have provided memorandums from the Special Agent on the case ( at least 30 victims ), sheriff report, had a check returned for fraudulent use, demanded to cancel 7 of the 8 checks before they were cashed- this did happen once the fraudulent check was returned, but still too late as 2 checks had already cleared their bank. 

As of yesterday, the XXXX  of XXXX XXXX XXXX  informed me, after 122 days after the checks were issued, I believe USPS acknowledged that they were having an issue with an employee that was stealing checks and cashing them. Have you followed up with them to see if they will advance these fees? 

I am a victim of theft and the closing company/banking provider is asking me to get remedy with the USPS? Seems very dismissive. They also now are saying possible theft after proving with both law enforcement statements but also the other returned check for fraud. It is our money we are paying interest on, but since the money was distributed through their banking account, my hands are tied and at this point with the response I am receiving, I doubt any action is actually being taken. 

Here is a detailed timeline of events : *On XX/XX/2022 between the XXXX and XXXX we purchased 8 priority mail envelopes at the XXXX XXXX approved USPS office XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX counter. The employee stamped each envelope with appropriate postage. ( {$8.00} an envelope ). 

XXXX XX/XX/2022 I called the XXXX USPS office as all 8 tracking numbers showed XXXX movement. Was informed no envelopes at the facility. Was referred to the USPS at XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX, XXXX XXXX. I phoned the USPS with XXXX resolutions. 

XXXX XX/XX/2022 reached out to XXXX XXXX  to cancel lost checks, as XXXX checks were processed as payment through creditors and XXXX movement reflected on tracking information - told must wait 30 days. 

XXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX XXXX  received returned check made to XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX {$1800.00} ) for fraudulent activity causing for bank to investigate the other XXXX checks. 

*Upon investigation checks made to XXXX XXXX {$2700.00} ) and XXXX XXXX XXXX {$2400.00} ) cleared the bank on XX/XX/2022 ( attached copies of endorsements ). 

*The other XXXX checks ( approximately {$40000.00} ) were able to be stop payment and reissued. ( The originals are still lost. ) *At this time the stolen checks that were cashed was a total of {$5100.00}. 

XXXX XXXX ( Friday ) a local police report was made with the XXXX XXXX. They informed me it was out of their jurisdiction but still made a report. Case XXXX XXXX XXXX ( Monday )XXXX Sheriff contacts us to let us know the theft is part of a larger criminal activity and I would be getting contacted by a special Agent from the Inspector General. ( All of this correspondence was forwarded to XXXX XXXX  ). 

XXXX XX/XX/XXXX, the memo expressing our checks were part of this crime, from Special Agent XXXX XXXX XXXX was forwarded to XXXX XXXX 

We followed up weekly and never could get a straight answer or who was working our file to help get our stolen funds back. 

After months of different staff being assigned our case and another follow up from us, I was asked on XX/XX/XXXX if we had any legal documentation to provide to help support our case of theft. 

As of yesterday the VP sent the email I referred to in the beginning of seeking remedy with the USPS. 

The overall complaint- we still havent been refunded for the stolen money and now the company is suggesting remedies that are not only ridiculous but insulting. We did not get services paid for by this company. We are paying double payments as the money was to pay off a credit card and now it is also on my mortgage

Consumer complaint narrative:
Macy 's Fraud Department contacted me by phone on XXXX XXXX, XXXX to verify suspicious charges and it turns out the charges were fraudulent. Macy 's XXXX XXXX removed a {$260.00} charge from my Macy 's store account and told me I should send a " Statement of Fraud '' to Macy 's American Express Fraud Department to remove a {$40.00} Shell Oil charge made in Delaware. I live in XXXX, FL and I have never been to Delaware. Also, I was told not to pay for the {$40.00} charge. That same day, I went to a Macy 's store in XXXX, Florida and paid off my balance of {$1300.00} in cash. 

Macy 's Fraud Department told me they were mailing a " Statement of Fraud '' to my home address ( they told me they could not e-mail or fax the form ). After several calls to the department I received said " Statement of Fraud '' on XXXX XXXX, XXXX ( 1 month after the initial call ). I signed it and return it by mail on XXXX XXXX, XXXX. I called the fraud department and I was told it would take several weeks to process the fraud investigation after they received my letter. I paid my XXXX Macy 's statement balance minus the {$40.00} Shell Oil charge. My headache was just starting. The Fraud department kept saying that they did not received my letter or my fax. So, I started calling and faxing as follows : Faxed on XXXX XXXX, XXXX Called and Faxed on XXXX XXXX, XXXX Called and Faxed on XXXX XXXX, XXXX Called and Faxed on XXXX XXXX, XXXX The answer was always the same : " there is no record of receiving the Statement of Fraud by either mail or fax therefore we can not investigate it. Whom It May Concern, I recently reviewed my credit report and noticed that your company is reporting a debt under the above-referenced account number. I do not recognize this debt and am formally disputing its validity under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ) and Regulation F ( 12 CFR Part 1006 ) Additionally, I am requesting full validation of the debt under my rights provided by the Fuir Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ).

Debt Validation Request Under Regulation F & FCRA To comply with federal regulations, I request that you provide : 1. Creditor Information : The name and address of both the original creditor and the current creditor wh
o owns the debt.

2. Debt Verification : A breakdown of the delt amount, including principal, interest, fees, and other charges as required under 12 CFR 1006.34 ( c ).

Proof of a valid contract or agreement establishing the alleged debt and my obligation to pay it.

3. Termination Date : Provide the specific reference date you used in your itemization ( such as the last statement date, charge-off date, or last payment date ).

4. Assignment Records : Copies of all assignments or transfers demonstrating the chain of ownership of this debt from the original creditor to your agency.

5. Proof of Permissible Purpose : Documentation explaining the permissible purpose under which you accessed and reported this debt to the credit bureaus, per 15 U.S.C. 1681b ( Fair Credit Reporting Act ), 6. Cease Collection Until Validation Is Provided : If you can not provide proper validation, I request that you immediately cease all collection activities and remove this account from my credit reort.

7. Credit Reporting Requirements : If this alleged debt is reported to the credit bureaus. I request that it be marked as " disputed by consumer '' per 15 U.S.C. 1681-2 ( a ) ( 3 ) If this debt can not be validated, I demand that it be deleted from my credit report immediately in accordance with the FCRA. 

I request confirmation that no negative information will be furnished to the credit bureaus during the validation process, per Regulation F guidance.

8. Notification to Future Collectors : If you transfer or sell this account to another agency, they must also be informed of my dispute per 12 CFR & 1006.30 ( d ) ( 1 ) Next Steps Please provide a written response to this request within the legally required timeframe. If you can not validate this debt, I expect you to promptly remove it from my credit report and cease all collection efforts. 

Additionally, I do not consent to any form of contact via email, phone, or text message. I request thar all communications be conducted strictly via mail. 

Thank you for your immediate attention to this matter.

Consumer complaint narrative:
This complaint is being filed against TransUnion for ongoing and willful violations of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) and for its failure to ensure the maximum possible accuracy of the information contained in my consumer report, as required under 15 U.S.C. 1681i. 
Since XX/XX/XXXX, I have submitted multiple disputes regarding inaccurate, and misleading information being reported by furnishers on my TransUnion report. Despite my good faith efforts and clear documentation, TransUnion continues to verify clearly false information, and new inaccuracies have appeared without correction of prior ones.XXXX XXXX is still reporting a credit limit on a charge-off account, which is inaccurate and misleading. TransUnion previously removed a XXXX tradeline for this exact same issue reporting a credit limit on a charge account. It is unreasonable and negligent that TransUnion fails to apply the same correction to XXXX XXXX XXXX is reporting a past due amount that increases every month, despite the account being closed. The current past due is listed as {$6200.00}, while the total balance is {$10000.00}. This discrepancy is illogical and suggests an intentional disregard for accurate reporting. A closed account should not show a growing balance or an inconsistent past due XXXX XXXX is still reporting a charge-off every month, giving the false appearance that the account is newly delinquent each month. In addition, they have reported a false closure date of XX/XX/XXXX, when the account was actually charged off three years ago. This is a textbook example of re-aging, a deceptive and illegal practice meant to extend the time derogatory information stays on my report. 
In addition to failing to correct these inaccuracies, TransUnion has violated 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( a ) ( 6 ) by refusing to send me the results of its investigations within five business days of completion. Instead, after I filed a complaint through the CFPB about this, TransUnion responded by demanding proof of address after the investigation was completed which is a deceptive practice. If there were identity verification issues, they were obligated to resolve that before beginning the investigation, not after. 
Further, the address TransUnion has on file is the same one used in my disputes, and my notarized disputes should serve as proof of my identity and address. Denying me access to my own investigation results is an additional violation of my consumer rights under federal law.TransUnion has demonstrated a pattern of negligence, noncompliance, and willful disregard for federal obligations. They have repeatedly failed to provide complete and accurate investigation results, failed to maintain maximum accuracy on my consumer report, and have allowed furnishers to manipulate and misreport critical information.This is my final warning and request for TransUnion to either Correct the reported inaccuracies, orDelete the affected tradelines entirely from my consumer report. 
I request the CFPB to investigate TransUnions repeated violations of the FCRA and to take immediate action to protect my rights as a consumer.

Consumer complaint narrative:
My complaint supports the below findings and is in direct violation of my rights as a PICK-A-PAYMENT mortgage scam victim. Please ref account known as XXXX. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, a federal judge found Wells Fargo Bank is in breach of a XXXX nationwide mortgage loan class action settlement involving adjustable payments for home mortgages, since the bank allegedly did not properly evaluate homeowners who applied for these types of loan payments in order to avoid foreclosure. 
U.S. District Judge XXXX XXXX asked Wells Fargo to meet with the plaintiffs in order to find a new way to fix the mortgage loan class action settlement violations, which will need to include steps that will allow homeowners to reapply for loan assistance through Wells Fargo Bank. 
Judge XXXX decision is just the most recent of a long list of disputes regarding the XXXX Wells Fargo mortgage loan class action settlement, an agreement which resolved several complaints related to the banks pick-a-payment loans, many of which were acquired by Wells Fargo during their XXXX acquisition of Wachovia Corp . 
These pick-a-payment loans allowed borrowers to initially pay less interest than was due on their mortgage ; however, the escalating payments that came later as a result of these types of mortgage loans contributed to the thousands of home foreclosures that occurred during the countrys XXXX housing crisis. For years the plaintiffs legal representatives argued Wells Fargo was in violation of the Wells Fargo mortgage loan class action settlement agreement, in that the bank was not granting loan modifications that would have been worth {$2.00} billion to homeowners with Wells Fargo pick-a-payment mortgage loans. These modifications were an integral part of the Wells Fargo mortgage loan settlement agreement, which also stipulated the bank would pay {$50.00} million to eligible Class Members. 
However, according to the plaintiffs new Wells Fargo mortgage loan class action lawsuit, the bank was not using proper methodologies to evaluate whether or not a homeowner was at risk of imminent default, which would have qualified them for loan assistance under the previous Wells Fargo mortgage loan class action settlement. 
The plaintiffs legal representatives in this mortgage loan class action lawsuit allege thousands of homeowners were denied the mortgage assistance due to them because Wells Fargo Bank used faulty methods to measure their financial hardships pertaining to the pick-a-payment home loans. 
In his recent ruling, Judge XXXX reprimanded both sides of this Wells Fargo mortgage loan class action lawsuit, stating that it seemed that neither side knew exactly what they agreed to more than four years ago, during the initial Wells Fargo mortgage loan class action settlement. However, the judge agreed with the plaintiffs arguments that the bank breached the settlement agreement by using evolving and ill-defined standards when determining homeowners loan modification applications. 
The court has ordered that both Wells Fargo and the suing plaintiffs present a joint or competing proposals to correct the mortgage loan class action settlement violations to be submitted within the next two weeks.

The Wells Fargo Mortgage Loan Class Action Lawsuit is In re : Wachovia Corp. Pick-A-Payment Mortgage Marketing and Sales Practices Litigation, Case No. XXXX, in the U.S. District Court for the XXXXXXXX District of California XXXX

If this debt has been forgiven, canceled, charged off or settled for less than the full balance, the IRS 1099-C form is required to be filed under 26 U.S.C. 6050P In addition to the 1099-C form, I am disputing the accuracy of the debt and requesting verification in compliance with the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), specifically 15 U.S.C. 1681i ( Reinvestigation of Disputed Information ), and 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( Responsibilities of Furnishers of Information ) . I am entitled to request and receive complete documentation to verify the accuracy and legality of this account 's reporting, and I am invoking my rights under [ Texas ] law to receive proper validation of this debt. Written Communication Only : Please be advised that I am requesting written communication only regarding this matter. I do not wish to receive any phone calls.. Any correspondence should be directed to the address listed above. This request is made pursuant to my right under 15 U.S.C. 1692c ( c ) of the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ), which entitles me to request that all communication from you be in writing. Requests for Documentation : 1. 1099-CForm : 2. If the debt has been discharged, forgiven, or settled for less than the full amount, I request that you provide a copy of the 1099-C form filed with the IRS. If you have not filed this form, or if you can not provide it, I will assume that the debt has been discharged and should no longer be reported as owed on my credit report. Failure to file the 1099-C may constitute fraudulent reporting of the debt. 3. Authenticated Double-Entry Accounting Records : In addition to the 1099-C, I am requesting full double-entry accounting records for the account in question. This should include a detailed breakdown that shows all transactions, including the original balance, payments, interest, fees, and any other adjustments made to the account. Please ensure that the records comply with standard accounting practices, and provide authenticated evidence of the debt, rather than a simple list of charges or account statements. The court ruling in XXXX v. XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX Cir. XXXX ), states that debt collectors must provide verified and authenticated documentation, not merely conclusory statements. 4. Proof of Legal Right to Report and Collect the Debt : I also request authenticated evidence, in compliance with [ Texas ] law, that you have the legal right to report and collect this debt. This should include the original signed contract or agreement showing my obligation to pay the debt and your legal right to enforce collection and report it to the credit bureaus. In XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX. XXXX XXXX. XXXX ), the court held that creditors must provide original signed documentation to prove the validity of a debt. Failure to provide this evidence could result in violations of both state and federal consumer protection laws. Request for Deletion if Evidence is Not Provided : If you are unable to provide the requested documentation or proof of the debt, I request that you immediately delete this account from my credit report in compliance with 15 U.S.C. 1681i and 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2. Under these provisions, you are required to investigate and correct any inaccuracies in the information you report to consumer reporting agencies. Failure to respond to this request, or failure to provide the required documentation, will compel me to take further action. I will file a formal complaint with the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ), pursue legal remedies, and, if applicable, file a Suspicious Activity Report ( SAR ) with the IRS under FinCEN Form 111 for potential fraud if you continue to report inaccurate or unverified information. Please respond within 5 days of receipt of this letter.

Consumer complaint narrative:
I am writing to bring attention to the provisions outlined in the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), specifically 15 U.S. Code 1681, Section 602 ( a ). This section emphasizes the critical role of consumer reporting agencies ( CRAs ) in our financial system and underscores the importance of treating consumers fairly, impartially, and with respect for their privacy rights. 
As both XXXX and XXXX operate as consumer reporting agencies, it is imperative that they adhere to the principles set forth in the FCRA. As a consumer, I assert my right to ensure that my private information remains confidential and is not shared without proper authorization. This right is supported by 15 U.S. Code 6801, which places an obligation on financial institutions to safeguard customer privacy.

Furthermore, according to 15 U.S. Code 1681, Section 604 ( a ) ( 2 ), consumer reporting agencies may only furnish consumer reports with explicit written consent. I have not provided such consent, and any previous consent granted to XXXX, XXXX, or other information furnishers to credit agencies is hereby revoked. 
Additionally, 15 U.S. Code 6802 ( b ) mandates that financial institutions inform consumers of their right to opt out of information disclosure. Regrettably, this requirement has not been met by XXXX and XXXX. 
Moreover, 15 U.S. Code 1681C ( a ) ( 5 ) explicitly prohibits consumer reporting agencies from including adverse items in reports without proper authorization. XXXX and XXXX must adhere to this standard.

Lastly, 15 U.S. Code 1681s-2 ( A ) ( 1 ) prohibits the furnishing of inaccurate information, while 15 U.S. Code 1681e requires consumer reporting agencies to maintain reasonable procedures to prevent violations. Unfortunately, it appears that XXXX and XXXX are falling short in meeting these standards. 
Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
Consumer complaint narrative:
I am filing a formal complaint against Experian for its repeated and willful violations of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ). For over a year, I have submitted multiple disputes and identity theft documentation to Experian regarding fraudulent accounts that were opened without my knowledge or consent. 

Despite my good faith efforts, Experian continues to allege that these accounts are valid, even though they are the result of identity theft. I have never opened or authorized these accounts, and there is no valid documentation, contract, or proof that ties these accounts to me. 



Fraudulent Accounts That Must Be Blocked and Deleted : The following accounts are not mine, and are the direct result of identity theft. They must be immediately blocked and permanently removed in accordance with FCRA XXXX : Account Name : XXXX XXXX XXXX Account Number : XXXX Account Name : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Account Number : XXXX Experians Legal Violations : 1. FCRA 605B ( 15 U.S.C. 1681c-2 ) Blocking of Information Resulting from Identity Theft Experian is required to block identity theft-related information within 4 business days of receiving a valid police report or FTC Identity Theft Report. I have submitted both, along with proof of identity. Experians failure to remove the accounts above is a clear violation. 

2. FCRA 611 ( 15 U.S.C. 1681i ) Failure to Conduct a Reasonable Investigation Experian must conduct a reasonable reinvestigation when a dispute is submitted. The continued reporting of these accounts with no verified proof indicates no reasonable investigation occurred.

3. FCRA 623 ( a ) ( 6 ) Failure to Mark Accounts as Disputed Experian failed to properly flag these accounts as being disputed due to fraud, as required under FCRA. 

4. FCRA 616 & 617 ( 15 U.S.C. 1681n & 1681o ) Civil Liability Experians negligent and willful refusal to comply with federal law exposes them to statutory and punitive damages, as well as attorney fees. 



Supporting Case Law : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX XXXX. XXXX ) : Credit bureau held liable for failing to remove inaccurate information after repeated consumer disputes. 
XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX. XXXX ) : Confirmed duty to block identity theft accounts under XXXX. 
XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX ) : Reaffirmed that credit bureaus must conduct independent investigations, not just rely on creditors. 
XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX ) : Failing to report an account as disputed is a material inaccuracy and violates the FCRA. 



My Position and Next Steps : I have now retained legal counsel and will pursue legal action if these accounts are not deleted within 4 business days. I am seeking : Permanent deletion of all identity theft-related accounts Written confirmation from Experian that these accounts will not be reinserted Statutory, actual, and punitive damages Attorneys fees and all legal remedies available Additionally, I will be submitting complaints to : Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) Federal Trade Commission ( FTC ) My State Attorney Generals Office XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ) Office of the Inspector General ( FTC ) Department of Consumer Affairs ( state-level ) Summary of Relief Demanded : 1. Delete the following accounts : XXXX XXXX XXXX Account # XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX Account # XXXX XXXX. Send written confirmation of deletion XXXX. Cease and desist from any reinsertion or future reporting of these accounts unless full proof of authorization is provided 4. Bring Experian into immediate compliance with FCRA 605B, 611, and 623 Failure to comply will result in litigation. Experian has had more than enough time to correct these issues. My previous CFPB complaints have gone ignored, which adds to the growing evidence of willful misconduct. It is now clear that Experian has chosen noncompliance as a business practice.

Issue: Advertising and marketing, including promotional offers
Sub-issue: Didn't receive advertised or promotional terms
Consumer complaint narrative: To Whom It May Concern, I am writing regarding my Gap Rewards Credit Card. I have been a Gap card holder for well over 10 years and the card was always under XXXX. Last year it converted to a Barclays card. I recently have experienced financial hardship and enrolled in a Debt Management Program through XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. XXXX sent a proposal to Barclays for both my Gap rewards card and my Banana Republic card. The proposal was to lower the interest rate from 29.99 % to 5.4 % and would have me pay off my balance in 4.5 years. In addition to this arrangement XXXX advised me to close the accounts, which I did. Barclays accepted the proposal for the Banana Republic card, however rejected the proposal for the Gap card. When XXXX as well as myself contacted Barclays to inquire the reason, we were told that I had a promotional interest rate on the account of 0 % until XX/XX/XXXX. I found this odd because all of my statements show that I am in fact paying 29.99 % interest. Upon further explanation, I was informed that the 0 % promotion only applies to purchases made between XX/XX/XXXX XX/XX/XXXX. Which total {$390.00}. My current balance on the card is {$7500.00} and that is being paid at a 29.99 % interest rate. I asked if there was a way to remove the promotion so that I may enroll in the program. I was told no. I asked multiple times if I could pay the {$390.00} over and beyond my minimum payment in order to make the promotional balance XXXX, so that I may enter the program. I was told no. In fact, 3 different representatives told myself and XXXX, that any payment above the minimum payment would be spread out among the balance and NOT directed specifically to the 0 % promotion. This means that I am in fact NOT paying 0 % on the {$390.00}. Barclays is not offering me any way to take advantage of this promotion by allowing me to pay it off prior to XX/XX/XXXX. The only way to pay off the {$390.00} is the pay the entire balance of {$7500.00}. This is unfair. XXXX stated that they have never heard of a creditor doing this and it is false advertising. I would dare say its predatory lending. They offered a promotion before the holidays offering 0 % interest on new purchases however never created a way for me to actually pay off that balance. If I go to XXXX and buy furniture for 0 % interest for 6 months, they absolutely allow me to direct funds ( over and beyond my minimum payment ) to the balance of that furniture. Why is there no way through this promotion, to get out of it? How do I ensure that I am paying 0 % if you will not allow me to pay the {$390.00} prior to XXXX? Lastly and additionally predatory, is that because of this promotion on {$390.00}, Barclays will not allow me to enter a program to address the 29.99 % interest on {$7000.00} balance. They are essentially tying me into this rate and payments for 6 more months. There is no way out. That is not a promotion and I spoke to 7 different representatives yesterday who all claimed there was nothing they could do. This is unacceptable. I am asking for the promotion to either be removed from my account, or for the opportunity to pay off the promotional balance of {$390.00} so as to not accrue any interest, which is how a true promotion is supposed to operate. I look forward to a response.

Please refer to the attached file detailing these items I am disputing.

The Fair Credit Reporting Act ( 15 U.S. Code 1681 ) says ( 1 ) The banking system is dependent upon fair and accurate credit reporting. Inaccurate credit reports directly impair the efficiency of the banking system, and unfair credit reporting methods undermine the public confidence, which is essential to the continued functioning of the banking system. So whenever there is a violation under the FCRA, it impairs the efficiency of the banking system. This is a violation of my privacy with my consumer report, since I control what is listed and what is not. In the event that any accounts are reported without my written consent, that is considered identity theft. ''

Consumer complaint narrative:
Hi. I'm wanting to let you know about a situation that just happened yesterday with Waypoint. Waypoint is a collection agency. They are collecting on XXXX however that company no longer exists. The company is now named XXXX XXXX XXXX. 

Today I checked my credit report and I saw Waypoint listed as 1 of only 2 collections. 1 of those collections I didn't recognize so I decided to dispute it but I did have a XXXXe account in the past so I decided I would at least call Waypoint to find out what was going on. I spoke to a woman named XXXX XXXX. She was a very nice collector. She informed me that this was for a bill for services. She informed me that according to the records on file, I last paid XX/XX/XXXX but services were cancelled on XX/XX/XXXX. She said this bill was for those charges. I know I'm not a perfect individual, but I remember cancelling services with XXXX and going in person to turn the equipment in almost immediately. I know for a fact that I asked at the moment of turning my equipment in if there were any additional fees. I was told no. As any person would do after being told they didn't have any money owed, I went about my business and moved on with my life. 

Here we are 2 years later and I check my XXXX XXXX to ensure that my credit report is accurate and I see this account on my report. I explained to XXXX that I didn't owe any money to XXXX ; and if I did, why didn't they ever bill me or call me about this debt? XXXX went on to explain that her company had sent me a bill this past XX/XX/XXXX. She said she sent it to a XXXX XXXX address. Unfortunately, I don't live at the XXXX XXXX address. I informed her of that fact and XXXX went on to explain that is why I didn't know about the bill. She said that is also why I didn't receive a bill from XXXX either. I could almost understand her logic but I had to correct her because it was based on a faulty premise. It is true that I no longer live at the XXXX XXXX address now, but I did live there for a little over a year from the date she stated I made my last payment in XX/XX/XXXX. I lived there until XX/XX/XXXX. In other words, I lived at XXXX XXXX 1 year and 1 month after I made my last payment to XXXX. That meant XXXX had ample opportunity to bill me or notify me in any way that I owed them money but they didn't. I explained that to XXXX and what I hear next is what really pushed me over the edge. 

Not only was I in collections for a bill that I never knew I owed, but XXXX went on to state that around that same time, XXXX was being bought out by XXXX XXXX/ XXXX and there was going to be delays and confusion. She said it took time for XXXX to figure out what was going on and then take over. I somewhat understood that so I offered her to pay this account off to avoid any lengthy disputes and investigations in exchange for having this account removed from my credit report due to no billing or by her own admission, billing errors. She refused. She stated that her company has to report accurately and what would be the point of the credit bureau if she removed this account. She also admitted that other companies delete accounts but her company doesn't. I then informed her that the reporting of this account is not accurate. Placing an account on my credit report as a collection is akin to letting the world know that I refused to pay a bill when that is not the case. By her own admission XXXX bought XXXX and it took them a while to dig through all the records and figure out what's going on. She also said that it seemed like XXXX threw their hands up during this time and pretty much gave up since they were being bought out. 

How am I supposed to accept this as a consumer? How am I supposed to accept this as a person? From my point of view, I'm being led to believe that because a company decided to sell itself to someone else then I am being held responsible for their lack of record keeping or improper billing. That I am going to for the next 7 years be labeled as a consumer that doesn't pay his bills to any future creditor. How is that fair? Because of that I asked to speak to a supervisor. I ended up being transferred to a XXXX XXXX. To make this complaint shorter, I went through the same conversation with her and also asked her to delete this account for the same reasons. XXXX refused because she claimed that she sent the mail trough a special means with the USPS that supposedly would have the mail reach me even if the mail was sent to a wrong address. Well it didn't. We went through a whole dance of me stating facts and her putting up abstract objections such as millions of people get their mail from the post office we don't know why you didn't or XXXX should have billed you we don't know why they didn't. In the end, she also refused to remove this account from my credit report even thought it was confirmed that XXXX was bought out by XXXX and the buyout caused record problems. It was confirmed that Waypoint sent mail to a wrong address. It was confirmed that Waypoint had never spoken to me about this debt. Citibank notified me towards the end of XXXX XXXX that my phone number and email address was deactivate for XXXX. Then on XX/XX/2022 they blocked my account without notice. I called them on XX/XX/2022 and inquired about my blocked account. 

They informed me that the bank had performed a review of my transactions and that they had made a decision to begin closing procedure on my account, due to my XXXX activity. They never informed me what was wrong with the transaction, nor were there any refund requests or reversals within my account that exhibited any risk to the bank, only stating that I would be receiving a letter explaining why my account was being closed. 

About a week later I received a letter in the mail from Citibank stating, " We periodically review customer accounts to access the suitability of their banking relationship with Citibank XXXX to a recent review, it has been determined that we can no longer service your account ending - XXXX and your other related accounts. Based on this decision, we have taken steps to terminate your banking relationship ( s ) with Citibank, pursuant to the terms of the Citibank Client Manual - Consumer Accounts. '' The letter went on to explain that my accounts would be restricted from any debits and certain credits and will be closed within sixty days from the date of the letter, which was XX/XX/2022. 

The letter continued to express several important points regarding what I need to do in order to not have additional funds tied up in the closure process. To include a paragraph regarding funds that are in the account that belong to me, that would be maile dto me in the form of a check upon account closure, and that I may visit my local branch or contact them at XXXX Option XXXX, XXXX to determine if I'm entitled to the funds sooner than the expected closure date. 

On several occasions I called the Fraud Prevention Department trying to find out why my account was being closed and if I could receive my funds sooner than 60 days, each time I was given no information nor an option to receive my balance of funds sooner. On two occasions when I called the representatives rudely stated they were terminating the call because they could not give me any information. I stated several times over a month and during at least 8 calls that I was experiencing a hardship since my Social Security and my Veteran 's Administration Pension was direct deposit to that account, unable to change to another bank account until XXXX. This left me without funds for food, rent and bills now for 2 months with their 60 days hold making it 3 months without any funds in XXXX. This has caused me undue hardship, stress, anxiety, inability to maintain my diet, sleep, buy medication and supplements for my XXXX XXXX, as well a putting me behind on my rent and bills.

Consumer complaint narrative: XXXX XXXX XXXX is in violation of multiple laws set forth by Congress in regards to the following account # XXXX. 15 U.S. Code 1681a ( 2 ) ( a ) ( i ) states that the consumer report does not include information solely as to transactions or experiences ( payment history, charge off status ), between the consumer ( myself ) and the person ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ) making the report. Additionally, 15 U.S. Code 1681 s-2 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( A ) states that A person ( US DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION ) shall not furnish any information relating to a consumer ( ME ) to any consumer reporting agency ( XXXX, Transunion, XXXX ) if the person knows or has reasonable cause to believe that the information is inaccurate. XXXX XXXX XXXX can not collect on a discharged debt. This is now considered a certificate of indebtedness per the IRS and income can not be reported. The IRS Clearly defines a charge off as Gross or Ordinary income, INCOME DOES NOT GET REPORTED ON THE CONSUMER REPORT which in fact makes reporting of this account inaccurate! I provided a screenshot from the IRS website. By Definition, the IRS Clearly says a Cancelled debt or Charge off is Income. The reporting of this account as a debt is inaccurate. I never received a 1099-C from XXXX XXXX XXXX for the cancelled debt of { { {$12000.00} } } in order to file as ordinary income. Where is my 1099-C XXXX XXXX XXXX is clearly writing off debt with the IRS without sending out 1099-C as required by the IRS for debts greater than { { {$600.00} } }. This is unsatisfactory! XXXX XXXX XXXX has been HEREBY PUT ON NOTICE that they are furnishing incorrect, inaccurate information. In XXXX XXXX XXXX terms and conditions they agreed not to share my information with non affiliates. XXXX, Transunion and XXXX are not an affiliate of XXXX XXXX XXXX nor are they " bureaus ''. The only true credit bureau is the CFPB and they DO NOT furnish consumer reports as stated in federal law. I have a right to privacy and Per the Privacy Act of 1974 as a federally protected consumer I am now revoking any and all authorization I, the consumer, may have given you written, non written, verbal, and nonverbal per 5 U.S. Code 552a. Per the FCRA, as a federally protected consumer, I am now opting out of any and all authorization I, the consumer, may have given you written, non written, verbal, and non verbal per 15 USC 6802. Furthermore, the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act ( FERPA ) is a federal law enacted in 1974 that protects the privacy of student education records from any public or private elementary, secondary, or post-secondary school. I shall also point out their responsibility pursuant to 15 U.S. Code section 1681s-2 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( B ) ( ii ) : ( a ) DUTY OF FURNISHERS OF INFORMATION TO PROVIDE ACCURATE INFORMATION XXXX ) PROHIBITION ( B ) Reporting information after notice and confirmation of errors A person shall not furnish information relating to a consumer to any consumer reporting agency if ( i ) the person has been notified by the consumer, at the address specified by the person for such notices, that specific information is inaccurate ; and ( ii ) the information is, in fact, inaccurate. XXXX XXXX XXXX must also refund the remaining balance of the consumer account upon its termination. This is your 7 day notice to return ALL money due from this consumer account. XXXX Treatment of credit balances ; account termination. ( a ) Credit balances. When a credit balance in excess of { { {$1.00} } } is created on a credit account ( through transmittal of funds to a creditor in excess of the total balance due on an account, through rebates of unearned finance charges or insurance premiums, or through amounts otherwise owed to or held for the benefit of the consumer ), the creditor shall : ( XXXX ) Credit the amount of the credit balance to the consumer 's account ; ( XXXX ) Refund any part of the remaining credit balance within seven business days from receipt of a written request from the consumer ; ( XXXX ) Make a good faith effort to refund to the consumer by cash, check, or money order, or credit to a deposit account of the consumer, any part of the credit balance remaining in the account for more than six months. No further action is required if the consumer 's current location is not known to the creditor and can not be traced through the consumer 's last known address or telephone number. ( b ) Account termination. ( XXXX ) A creditor shall not terminate an account prior to its expiration date solely because the consumer does not incur a finance charge. ( XXXX ) Nothing in paragraph ( b ) ( XXXX ) of this section prohibits a creditor from terminating an account that is inactive for three or more consecutive months. An account is inactive for purposes of this paragraph if no credit has been extended ( such as by purchase, cash advance or balance transfer ) and if the account has no outstanding balance. ( c ) Timely settlement of estate debts ( XXXX ) General rule. ( i ) Reasonable policies and procedures required. For credit card accounts under an open-end ( not home-secured ) consumer credit plan, card issuers must adopt reasonable written policies and procedures designed to ensure that an administrator of an estate of a deceased account holder can determine the amount of and pay any balance on the account in a timely manner. ( ii ) Application to joint accounts. Paragraph ( c ) of this section does not apply to the account of a deceased consumer if a joint account holder remains on the account. ( XXXX ) Timely statement of balance ( i ) Requirement. Upon request by the administrator of an estate, a card issuer must provide the administrator with the amount of the balance on a deceased consumer 's account in a timely manner. ( ii ) Safe harbor. For purposes of paragraph ( c ) ( XXXX ) ( i ) of this section, providing the amount of the balance on the account within 30 days of receiving the request is deemed to be timely. ( XXXX ) Limitations after receipt of request from administrator ( i ) Limitation on fees and increases in annual percentage rates. After receiving a request from the administrator of an estate for the amount of the balance on a deceased consumer 's account, a card issuer must not impose any fees on the account ( such as a late fee, annual fee, or over-the-limit fee ) or increase any annual percentage rate, except as provided by 1026.55 ( b ) ( 2 ). ( ii ) Limitation on trailing or residual interest. A card issuer must waive or rebate any additional finance charge due to a periodic interest rate if payment in full of the balance disclosed pursuant to paragraph ( c ) ( XXXX ) of this section is received within 30 days after disclosure.
